excerpt: [Legion Agents Index]|root: {AGENTS_DIR} (resolve via workflow-common-core Agent Path Resolution)
issue: Dynamic Knowledge Index tells readers to Read exact agent files, but the root uses the placeholder {AGENTS_DIR} with only a parenthetical resolution note. Under 4.7 literalism a reader may treat {AGENTS_DIR} as a literal path, or fail silently when workflow-common-core isn't loaded.
remediation_sketch: State the resolution rule inline and the fallback path. If resolution yields no concrete path, emit an escalation of type scope rather than guessing the agent directory.

excerpt: Marketing workflows activate when /legion:plan detects a marketing-focused phase (MKT-* requirements or marketing keywords). ... Design workflows activate when /legion:plan detects a design-focused phase (DSN-* requirements or design keywords).
issue: 'marketing keywords' and 'design keywords' are unenumerated triggers. Under 4.7 literalism, readers reproducing this decision may over-match on any surface keyword or miss synonyms not in a canonical list.
remediation_sketch: Drop 'or keywords' and restrict trigger to the MKT-*/DSN-* prefix, or cross-reference .planning/config/intent-teams.yaml as the authoritative trigger list.

excerpt: [Legion Agents Index]|root: {AGENTS_DIR} (resolve via workflow-common-core Agent Path Resolution)
issue: Parity finding with LEGION-47-001. AGENTS.md is byte-identical to CLAUDE.md for this surface. Since Codex CLI and other tools read AGENTS.md as primary root context, the {AGENTS_DIR} placeholder without resolution fallback has parity exposure on those adapters.
remediation_sketch: Apply identical fix to both CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md in a single edit batch. Consider generating both from a single source to prevent drift.

excerpt: Marketing workflows activate when /legion:plan detects a marketing-focused phase (MKT-* requirements or marketing keywords). ... Design workflows activate when /legion:plan detects a design-focused phase (DSN-* requirements or design keywords).
issue: Parity finding with LEGION-47-002. Unenumerated keyword triggers.
remediation_sketch: Apply identical fix to both files.

excerpt: | **Marketing Workflows** | MKT-* requirements or marketing keywords in phase | ... | | **Design Workflows** | DSN-* requirements or design keywords in phase | ...
issue: Parity with LEGION-47-002/004. README's Optional Features table repeats the 'marketing keywords'/'design keywords' trigger language. Severity is P3 here because README is a user-facing document rather than a persistent root-context file; behavioral risk is documentary. Should be remediated in lockstep with the CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md fix so all three surfaces agree.
remediation_sketch: Apply the same fix as LEGION-47-002/004.

excerpt: Verify the Legion commands appear with their canonical /legion:* names. ... Trigger the prompt and confirm it reads the installed .legion/commands/legion/start.md. ... Ask Cursor in plain language to use Legion start and confirm it reads .legion/commands/legion/start.md.
issue: Every checklist item uses a 'verify/confirm/trigger' verb without stating an observable pass criterion. For a human this is readable; for a 4.7-literal agent running the checklist (e.g., via a future /legion:validate dispatch), acceptance criteria are under-specified. 'Canonical /legion:* names' depends on an unlisted enumeration; 'reads the installed start.md' has no programmatic success signal; 'ask in plain language' does not specify a prompt string.
remediation_sketch: Add a 'Pass criteria' sub-bullet under each item stating an observable signal (e.g., running `/help` lists every name in a linked canonical set). For 'plain-language' prompts, include the exact prompt string or reference a fixture. Alternatively add a header disclaimer that the checklist is for human operators only and that programmatic validation lives in a separate harness.

excerpt: orchestrator_mediation: ... protocol: - Agent checks handoff context for expected inputs - If input is missing or ambiguous, agent documents an open_question - Agent proceeds with best-effort approach using available context - Open questions are surfaced in SUMMARY.md for resolution
issue: The third protocol step instructs the agent to proceed with best-effort approach without defining it. Under 4.7 literalism this supports at least four divergent interpretations (stall, speculate, conservative default, escalate). Different Wave 2+ agents under 4.7 will land in different buckets, producing inconsistent cross-wave behavior on the exact orchestration failure this protocol is meant to handle.
remediation_sketch: Replace the best-effort bullet with a small decision tree: apply conservative default where one exists (document in open_question), halt the specific task with warning escalation where none exists, continue with other in-scope tasks. Keeps non-blocking spirit but gives 4.7 a deterministic rule per missing-input case.

excerpt: exclusive_domains overlap across agents: data-pipelines (ai-engineer L80, data-analytics-engineer L590); experimentation (rapid-prototyper L105, growth-hacker L305); resource-allocation (sprint-prioritizer L348, studio-producer L410); creative-direction (whimsy-injector L273, studio-producer L409). conflict_resolution.same_domain_multiple_agents rule depends on specificity tiebreak but overlaps live at identical specificity level.
issue: Multiple agents declare overlapping values in exclusive_domains despite the field name and the file's opening description advertising exclusivity. The tie-break rule (higher specificity beats general) does not resolve same-level ties. Under 4.7 literalism a review-loop agent picking the domain owner for an overlapping domain will select whichever agent appears first in prompt order, nondeterministic across runs/CLIs/wave orderings, undermining the exclusive-domain invariant.
remediation_sketch: Preferred: disambiguate domain names per agent (e.g. ai-data-pipelines vs analytics-data-pipelines) so exclusive remains true. Alternate: add a shared_domains: section declaring intentional co-ownership with primary/secondary ordering. Stopgap: extend conflict_resolution with a deterministic tiebreaker for same-level ties (e.g. lexicographic id).

excerpt: "category": { "type": "string" }
issue: `category` is a free-form string with no enum, no pattern, and no description. Review findings are authored by QA/review agents; under 4.7 literalism the absence of an enumerated category set invites divergent category inventions across agents and cycles, undermining downstream aggregation in review-loop cycle-over-cycle diff tracking. Severity P3 because the schema is otherwise well-enumerated (severity, status both closed enums) and no observed reliance on category normalization exists yet.
remediation_sketch: Add an enum listing canonical finding categories (e.g. correctness, security, performance, style, testing, docs, other), or add a description pointing to skills/review-evaluators/SKILL.md as the authoritative enumeration. Enum preferred; matches the pattern used for severity and status.

excerpt: escalations.items.properties.type: { "type": "string" } (no enum) while severity sibling has enum [info,warning,blocker]
issue: escalations[].type is an un-enumerated free string, but CLAUDE.md Authority Matrix and .planning/config/escalation-protocol.yaml both define type as a closed 8-value set: architecture|dependency|scope|schema|api|deletion|infrastructure|quality. Schema drifts from documented protocol. Confirmed because divergence is already observable: validator accepts arbitrary values while the protocol file does not. Peer alignment issue with LEGION-47-008.
remediation_sketch: Add enum [architecture,dependency,scope,schema,api,deletion,infrastructure,quality] to escalations[].type. Fix alongside LEGION-47-008 and any CLAUDE.md edits so schema + protocol + root doc agree on the closed set.

excerpt: Present recommendation to user via adapter.ask_user: 'Which agent should advise on this topic?' Options: '{top_agent_id}' (Recommended), '{second_agent_id}'. ... e. If user selects 'Other': accept a custom agent ID
issue: AskUserQuestion call lists exactly two options without closure language and without an 'Other' option, but step (e) handles an 'Other' branch that was never offered. 4.7-literal interpreters will either invent a third option, drop the Other handler, or loosen to free text — all diverge from intent. Confirmed open-set anti-pattern.
remediation_sketch: Add 'Other (specify agent ID)' as an explicit third option so step (e) is reachable, or drop step (e) and lock to the two recommendations. Add closure header 'Choose one of the three options below; do not propose alternatives.'

excerpt: Use adapter.ask_user with a free-text prompt: 'What's your follow-up question?' Options: 'Type your question' (description: 'The same advisor will respond'), 'End session'
issue: Misuses AskUserQuestion: 'Type your question' is a label for free-text capture, not a selectable option. Under 4.7 literalism the call is internally inconsistent — the comment says free-text while Options enumerates labeled choices. Either renders as a forced choice between labels or escapes AskUserQuestion entirely (violating CLAUDE.md mandate).
remediation_sketch: Split into two steps: AskUserQuestion with closed options 'Continue with a follow-up' / 'End session'; if Continue, capture free text via adapter's text-input primitive. Do not mix labeled-option and free-text semantics in one call.

excerpt: Spawn the SAME agent again with updated prompt that includes: ... 'Review your previous advice (summarized below) and address this follow-up:\n{brief summary of prior advice}'
issue: 'brief summary of prior advice' is unbounded — no length cap, no structure, no extractor specified. 4.7 will produce summaries of widely varying length and shape across runs, breaking follow-up continuity.
remediation_sketch: Specify the summary contract: extract Recommendations section verbatim plus first sentence of each other section, capped at 400 words. If no Recommendations section, include prior response verbatim.

excerpt: Confirm via adapter.ask_user: 'I'll create a {division} agent — {suggested-name}. Specialty: {description}. Correct, or would you like to adjust?' If user adjusts: update inferred values and re-confirm
issue: Natural-language confirmation prompt with no enumerated options. AskUserQuestion requires explicit options; without them 4.7 invents options or renders raw text (violating CLAUDE.md mandate). The 'adjust' branch is undefined — name? division? specialty? Same pattern at lines 58 and 64.
remediation_sketch: Replace with explicit options: 'Create as-is' / 'Adjust name' / 'Adjust division' / 'Adjust specialty' / 'Restart'. On adjust branches, follow with targeted free-text capture. Apply same fix to lines 58, 64.

excerpt: Skip questions already answered in Stage 1 ... The 3-stage conversation is adaptive — skip questions the user already answered
issue: 'Already answered' has no operational definition. 4.7 must decide whether passing mentions or inferences count as answers; runs diverge between over-asking and over-skipping. 'adaptive' keyword is itself a CAT-2 trigger (branching without explicit conditions).
remediation_sketch: Define answered-ness: a question is answered when prior text contains a complete sentence addressing the question's core noun AND is at least 10 words. State the rule at the top of Stage 2.

excerpt: Use AskUserQuestion: question: 'What topic should the board deliberate on?' options: [] (free-text entry — no fixed options) Store the user's response as the topic.
issue: AskUserQuestion invoked with empty options array. Tool contract requires options. 4.7 will reject the call, inject defaults, or escape to raw text — all violate intent. The parenthetical admits the contract violation but does not resolve it. Same pattern as advise.md L155-160.
remediation_sketch: Two-step flow: AskUserQuestion with closed options 'Provide a topic' / 'Cancel'; if Provide, capture topic via adapter text-input primitive (not AskUserQuestion). Document the primitive in adapter spec.

excerpt: Select top 5–7 candidates from diverse divisions ... options: 'Recommended slate' — {agent1}..{agent5}, 'Custom composition' ... If 'Recommended slate': use the 5 recommended agents
issue: Candidate pool is 5–7 but slate option lists exactly 5 placeholders and resolution hard-codes 5; the 6th and 7th candidates are unreachable. AskUserQuestion has no closure language — 4.7 may invent options like 'Modified slate'. Confirmed open-set anti-pattern.
remediation_sketch: Reconcile to fixed count (5) or extend slate literal to {agent1}..{agentN} with N derived from candidate count. Add third option 'Show alternative slate (next-ranked candidates)'. Add closure header 'Choose one of the three options below; do not propose alternatives.'

excerpt: Collect all results; treat non-response after timeout as ABSTAIN with note
issue: Timeout duration unspecified. 4.7 dispatchers will pick different timeouts per run, producing inconsistent ABSTAIN counts and undermining vote integrity. Short timeouts bias toward ABSTAIN; long timeouts stall MEET mode.
remediation_sketch: Specify timeout: 'treat non-response after 180 seconds as ABSTAIN, with note: agent timed out — original prompt logged for replay'. Or delegate to adapter.timeout_assessment with a documented default.

excerpt: Gate: Architecture Review - Show analysis findings to user - Ask: 'Proceed to Wave B, or revise Wave A outputs?' If proceed: Generate Wave A manifest. If revise: Pause, user fixes, re-run Wave A
issue: Core two-wave gate prompt is free-text with bounded two-branch set but no AskUserQuestion wrapper, no enumerated options, no closure language. 4.7 may render raw text (violating CLAUDE.md mandate), invent a third option, or treat rhetorically and auto-proceed. High severity: this gate is the architectural safety rail for two-wave mode.
remediation_sketch: Replace with AskUserQuestion: options 'Proceed to Wave B' / 'Revise Wave A outputs' / 'Abort phase'. Add closure header 'Choose one of the three options below; do not propose alternatives.'

excerpt: User Confirmation (yolo mode skip) - Display: 'Detected intents: harden, skip-frontend' - Display: 'Execution mode: ad_hoc' or 'Execution mode: filter_plans' - Prompt: 'Proceed? [Y/n]'
issue: Raw [Y/n] prompt directly violates CLAUDE.md mandate ('you MUST use the AskUserQuestion tool. No exceptions'). 4.7 may render literal text, auto-assume yes per [Y/n] default convention, or wrap with ad-hoc options that differ run-to-run. 'yolo mode skip' is undefined parenthetical with no skip condition specified.
remediation_sketch: Replace with AskUserQuestion: options 'Proceed with these intents' / 'Cancel and adjust flags'. Remove [Y/n] convention. Define yolo mode as a named setting or delete the parenthetical.

excerpt: Ask user: 'Re-run all plans (including completed ones), or skip to incomplete plans only?' ... Use adapter.ask_user: 'Ready to execute Phase {N}: {phase_name}?' Options: 'Execute all plans', 'Execute specific wave only' — ask which wave number, 'Cancel'
issue: Line 228 is free-text without AskUserQuestion wrapper or closure. Lines 243-247's three-option call is properly structured but the 'Execute specific wave only' branch then 'ask which wave number' inline — the follow-up wave-number capture has no specification (integer? free text? bounded to discovered waves?). Nested prompt inherits lack of closure.
remediation_sketch: Line 228: AskUserQuestion with closed options 'Re-run all plans' / 'Skip to incomplete plans only' / 'Cancel'. Lines 243-247: after 'Execute specific wave only', follow with a second AskUserQuestion whose options are enumerated wave numbers from Step 2 plus 'Cancel'.

excerpt: activates two-wave mode when: 1. Phase has >= 4 plans 2. Plans span multiple service groups OR include explicit analysis tasks ... Detect analysis plans - Plans with wave_role: analysis in frontmatter - Plans assigned to architecture or security agents - Plans with 'review', 'audit', 'analyze' in objective
issue: Trigger set mixes three detection methods (frontmatter tag, agent-registry role, objective substring) without stating precedence or conjunction. 4.7 may require all three, any one, or apply unstated priority. Substring check 'review|audit|analyze in objective' will match any use of these words in objective prose (e.g., 'review the input for validity'). Suspected because the substring set is at least bounded to three exact tokens.
remediation_sketch: State combinator: 'A plan is analysis if ANY of: (a) frontmatter wave_role: analysis, (b) Agent: resolves to testing division or specific security/architect agent ids, (c) <objective> FIRST SENTENCE begins with Review|Audit|Analyze case-insensitive, word-boundary only. Do not match elsewhere.'

excerpt: Execute plans for this wave (wave-executor Section 4, Step 4): Follow the adapter-conditional execution from wave-executor: - If adapter.parallel_execution: spawn all agents simultaneously - If not: execute plans sequentially - Each agent uses adapter.model_execution
issue: Dispatch underspecified at two levels. (1) 'Spawn all agents simultaneously' lacks fan-out instruction — 4.7 defaults to fewer subagents, so without explicit 'issue all N spawns in a SINGLE tool call' the agent serializes spawns even when adapter supports parallel. (2) Instruction omits file-overlap safety check (files_modified collision detection). A reader of build.md alone is missing the prerequisites.
remediation_sketch: Expand: 'If adapter.parallel_execution AND no two plans in this wave share any files_modified entry: issue all plan spawns in a SINGLE tool call (fan-out = plan count; do not reduce). Otherwise: sequential in wave order with commit between each.' Cross-reference wave-executor Section 4 Step 4.

excerpt: Use adapter.ask_user with structured choices: 'Which exploration mode fits your goal?' → Crystallize (default): Transform a raw idea into a clear project concept / Onboard / Compare / Debate. Store selected mode as mode: crystallize|onboard|compare|debate
issue: Mode selection uses a decorative-arrow code block instead of an explicit AskUserQuestion options array. Four modes are a closed set (restriction on line 52 confirms) but presentation uses Unicode arrows and indentation rather than tool options contract. 4.7 may render as literal text, miss indented continuations, or re-format. 'Crystallize (default)' parenthetical adds ambiguity.
remediation_sketch: Replace with explicit AskUserQuestion: options ['Crystallize','Onboard','Compare','Debate'] with descriptions. Handle default semantics separately — auto-route empty input to Crystallize before the call. Add header 'Choose one of the four modes; do not propose alternatives.'

excerpt: Display: 'What are you exploring? Give me the raw idea'; Wait for user's input ... Display: 'What alternatives are you evaluating?' ... Display: 'What question or decision are you exploring?' ... 3. Confirm transition: 'Ready to run /legion:start with this crystallized concept?' Option 1: 'Yes, start planning' / Option 2: 'Review the summary first' / Option 3: 'Explore a different idea'
issue: Two anti-patterns. (1) Lines 60/84/89: 'Display...Wait for input' is free-text capture without AskUserQuestion wrapper, violating CLAUDE.md mandate. Line 61 claims 'ONE open input' but Compare/Debate repeat the pattern, contradicting the claim. (2) Lines 303-307's 'Option 1/2/3' is prose description, not a tool call — 4.7 renders as raw text.
remediation_sketch: For free-text modes: document adapter text-input primitive requirement, OR convert to two-step AskUserQuestion ('Provide topic' / 'Cancel') then free-text. For lines 303-307: replace 'Option 1/2/3' prose with explicit AskUserQuestion tool invocation with three options.

excerpt: Intervention triggers - Only intervene if: - Polymath asks an open-ended question (violation) → Correct - User is stuck for >60 seconds → Offer: 'Need help deciding?' - Exchange count reaches 7 without decision point → Remind Polymath
issue: Three triggers with no operational test. (a) 'Open-ended question' has no classifier; runs diverge on identical Polymath output. (b) 'Stuck for >60 seconds' references unobservable state — no CLI adapter surfaces per-turn wall-clock to orchestrator. (c) 'Exchange count reaches 7' is observable but reporting contract is unspecified. Command will intervene inconsistently or never.
remediation_sketch: (a) Define open-ended as 'final turn ends with ? and has no markdown list with 2+ items prefixed [A]/[B]/-'. (b) Delete the timer trigger or delegate to an adapter primitive. (c) Require Polymath to emit literal 'Exchange N of 7' marker; count highest N.

excerpt: Validate: if concept is extremely vague (less than 5 words), ask for slightly more detail
issue: 'Extremely vague' is operationalized ('<5 words') but rationale is unstated and 'slightly more detail' re-prompt is unbounded. 4.7 will produce varying re-prompts. 5-word threshold conflicts with example inputs (lines 65-68) where some fall on the edge. Suspected — behavior impact is minor.
remediation_sketch: Specify re-prompt: 'If concept has <5 whitespace-separated tokens, respond with exactly: Add one or two more details about the user, the problem, or the scope.' Or drop the word-count check and let Polymath surface vagueness.

excerpt: Pattern: Signals 'always','use','prefer','works well','best practice', positive framing. Pitfall: Signals 'don't','never','avoid','causes','breaks','watch out', negative framing. Preference: Signals 'we use','prefer X over Y','convention','standard','our approach'.
issue: Two defects. (1) 'prefer' appears in Pattern AND Preference signals — a lesson containing 'prefer' matches both and command specifies no tie-break. 4.7 picks first-listed type deterministically, misclassifying Preferences written as 'I prefer X'. (2) 'positive framing'/'negative framing' are not operational — no token-level polarity classifier specified. Relates to S02c cross-cut: inlined keyword registry with overlap.
remediation_sketch: Move 'prefer' to Preference-only. Delete 'positive/negative framing' tokens. Add precedence rule: 'If multiple types match, classify as most-specific: Preference > Pitfall > Pattern.' Document precedence; user-confirmation at lines 82-90 remains the safety net.

excerpt: Present classification via adapter.ask_user: 'I classified this as a {type}. Correct?' Options: 'Correct' — 'Save as {type}', 'It's a pattern', 'It's a pitfall', 'It's a preference'. ... If 'Yes': prompt for the next lesson text and return to Step 4
issue: Two defects. (1) Four options but one type-option always duplicates 'Correct' (whichever type was classified has two selectable paths). 4.7 tool call is internally inconsistent. (2) Line 143's 'prompt for the next lesson text' has no AskUserQuestion wrapper, contradicting line 246 self-claim that all user questions use adapter.ask_user.
remediation_sketch: (1) Collapse to three options: 'Confirm: {type}' / 'No — pick different type' / 'Cancel'. On 'No', follow with second AskUserQuestion with remaining two types. (2) For line 143: document adapter text-input primitive OR two-step flow.

excerpt: Tags: Extract relevant keywords - Technical terms (e.g., 'migrations','auth','API','CSS') - Domain terms (e.g., 'testing','deployment','design') - Generate 2-5 tags. Summary: Generate a one-line summary (max 80 characters)
issue: 'Technical terms' vs 'domain terms' have no operational distinction — 'API' could be either; examples overlap. 4.7 tag sets vary run-to-run, breaking --recall which scores by exact tag match. Two runs of same lesson produce different tags. Bounded to recall-quality degradation.
remediation_sketch: Drop Technical/Domain split. Replace with: 'Extract 2-5 tags. Each tag is one lowercase word, verbatim from lesson, >=3 chars, not a stopword.' Summary: 'first 80 chars of lesson, stopping at nearest word boundary. Do not generate new phrasing.'

excerpt: Present via adapter.ask_user: 'Save retrospective findings to memory?' Options: 'Save to memory' — 'Record findings to .planning/memory/RETRO.md for future planning context', 'View only (don't save)' — 'Retrospective displayed but not persisted', 'Edit before saving' — 'Make adjustments to findings before recording'
issue: Closed 3-option set formatted as em-dash prose pairs, not structured AskUserQuestion items. No 'only valid choices' closure. 4.7 may invent a fourth option or respond conversationally. Matches S03 closed-set-enforcement failure class across advise/board/build.
remediation_sketch: Wrap in explicit AskUserQuestion tool invocation with 3 items. Add closure sentence prohibiting option invention. Move descriptions into AskUserQuestion description field.

excerpt: Present the condensed findings via adapter.ask_user: 'Which sections need changes?' Options: 'What Went Well' / 'What Didn't Work' / 'Patterns' / 'Action Items' / 'Looks good — save as-is'
issue: Five options as slash-separated inline prose — decorative-menu anti-pattern (cf. explore.md L44-53). 4.7 cannot reliably parse slashes into AskUserQuestion items. Single-select vs. multi-select unspecified; users may need to edit multiple sections.
remediation_sketch: Convert to structured AskUserQuestion with 5 items. Specify iterative single-select loop until 'Looks good' is chosen, or enable multi_select.

excerpt: If invoked from /legion:portfolio context (detected via portfolio state or $ARGUMENTS containing --portfolio)
issue: 'Portfolio state' has no operational definition — no file path, no key, no value. 4.7 will either always take the false branch or invent a check. Only the $ARGUMENTS half is observable.
remediation_sketch: Replace disjunction with single observable condition tied to a concrete file path and field from the portfolio command spec. Drop the untestable half.

excerpt: Based on milestone state, determine available actions: Always available: View milestone details, Done. If In Progress + all phases Complete: Complete milestone. If Complete not Archived: Archive milestone. If milestones need redefinition: Redefine. Present options via adapter.ask_user: What would you like to do?
issue: AskUserQuestion option list computed dynamically from conditional branches but the prompt does not enumerate the final list — the agent assembles it. 4.7 may include all conditional options regardless of predicate, omit 'Done', or invent 'Cancel'. No closure framing.
remediation_sketch: Replace dynamic construction with an explicit predicate-to-option table and a pre-send assertion enumerating the final list. Forbid invented 'Cancel'; 'Done' serves that role. Inline a concrete example.

excerpt: Options: 'Archive' / 'Cancel'
issue: Slash-separated inline options — decorative-menu anti-pattern on a destructive file-move operation. Does not match AskUserQuestion tool call shape mandated by CLAUDE.md.
remediation_sketch: Wrap in explicit AskUserQuestion with two items and closure sentence prohibiting option invention.

excerpt: Ask via adapter.ask_user: 'Accept these milestone groupings?' Options: 'Accept' — 'Use these milestone definitions', 'Modify' — 'Let me adjust the groupings'. If 'Modify': Ask for specific changes (which phases to regroup, new names, etc.)
issue: Two defects. (1) Accept/Modify bounded set has no closure framing. (2) 'Ask for specific changes' at L233 is free-text capture, inheriting the S03 adapter.prompt_free_text contract defect (AskUserQuestion requires non-empty options). Parallel to LEGION-47-029 (learn.md L143).
remediation_sketch: Wrap L227-232 in explicit AskUserQuestion. For L233 decompose 'specific changes' into bounded sub-questions OR wait for documented adapter.prompt_free_text primitive. Cross-reference S03 upstream contract decision.

excerpt: Analyze for logical groupings: Look for theme clusters (infrastructure, features, integrations, etc.). Consider dependency chains. Aim for 2-4 milestones with 3-7 phases each.
issue: Three soft guidance items with no operational definition. 'Theme clusters' has no classifier. 'Consider dependency chains' does not specify how to consult dependencies. Bounds 2-4×3-7 conflict if project has 10 phases. Run-to-run groupings will vary.
remediation_sketch: Replace with deterministic procedure based on explicit phase-dependency column or keyword-cluster shifts; or hand groupings to the user via bounded AskUserQuestion rather than having 4.7 infer.

excerpt: Present options via adapter.ask_user: 'What would you like to do?' Options: 'View project details', 'Add dependency', 'Studio Producer analysis', 'Done'
issue: Closed 4-option set formatted as em-dash prose pairs, not structured AskUserQuestion items. No closure framing. Identical failure pattern to LEGION-47-031 (retro.md), LEGION-47-034 (milestone.md).
remediation_sketch: Wrap in explicit AskUserQuestion tool invocation with four items. Descriptions in description field. Add closure sentence prohibiting option invention.

excerpt: Ask user via adapter.ask_user: 'Which project is the source (blocker)?' ... Ask: 'Which phase in {source_project} must complete first?' ... Ask: 'Dependency type?' — Options: 'blocks (hard)' / 'informs (soft)'
issue: Five sequential Ask steps with inconsistent contracts. Phase-selection asks are free-text (inherits S03 adapter.prompt_free_text contract defect; parallel to LEGION-47-036 milestone.md L233 and LEGION-47-029 learn.md L143). Dependency-type ask uses slash-separated decorative-menu format.
remediation_sketch: (1) For phase asks: enumerate phase numbers from ROADMAP.md Progress table and pass as AskUserQuestion options. (2) Wrap dependency-type in AskUserQuestion with two items plus closure. (3) Or wait for adapter.prompt_free_text primitive.

excerpt: Green [OK]: on track, no blockers, recent activity. Yellow [!!]: 'escalated'/'failed'/'blocked' in status, or 7+ days inactive. Red [XX]: directory missing (Stale), or unresolved blockers 3+ phases
issue: 'On track' and 'recent activity' are not operationalized; conflicts with '7+ days inactive'. Timestamp source not specified. 'Unresolved blockers 3+ phases' is ambiguous (3+ affected vs 3+ consecutive vs 3+ back). Inline summary risks drift from authoritative portfolio-manager Section 3.
remediation_sketch: Replace with deterministic classifier referencing exact STATE.md fields and ROADMAP.md blocker counts. Delete inline summary or CI-check parity with skill file.

excerpt: Present recommendation to user via adapter.ask_user: 'Which agent should handle this task?' Options: top_agent (Recommended), second_agent, 'No agent — run autonomously'. e. If user selects 'Other': accept a custom agent ID from user input
issue: Structural contradiction: Step 3.d enumerates 3 options; Step 3.e references an 'Other' branch never offered. 4.7 cannot reach 3.e via strict AskUserQuestion contract. Failure modes: silently drop branch, invent fourth option, or accept free text. P1 because agent selection is the load-bearing decision in /legion:quick.
remediation_sketch: Add fourth 'Other — pick a different agent' option. On selection, second AskUserQuestion with agent-registry Section 1 IDs paginated by division. Delete or re-tie dangling 3.e. Wrap 3.d in explicit AskUserQuestion with closure.

excerpt: Use adapter.ask_user: 'Commit the changes from this quick task?' Options: 'Yes — commit with conventional message' (Recommended), 'No — leave uncommitted'
issue: Two-option set with em-dash prose format, no closure framing. Matches repeating failure pattern across S03/S04.
remediation_sketch: Wrap in explicit AskUserQuestion with 2 items plus closure prohibiting option invention.

excerpt: 'Review found blocking issues. How to proceed?' Options: 'Fix and retry' — 'Apply reviewer suggestions, then re-review' → Re-spawn ..., 'Proceed anyway' — 'Create PR with known issues noted' → Continue ..., 'Abort' — 'Stop here, I fix manually'
issue: Three options with em-dash descriptions and arrow-prefixed action notes inline. 4.7 may treat → lines as part of option text. Gate sits on fix-mode review-fail critical path; mis-selection either ships broken code or silently aborts.
remediation_sketch: Wrap in explicit AskUserQuestion; move arrow lines to separate Branch handling subsection keyed by returned option string.

excerpt: Determine commit type from task description: FIX_MODE or task mentions 'fix','bug','repair' -> fix(legion); 'test','spec' -> test(legion); 'doc','readme','comment' -> docs(legion); 'refactor','clean','reorganize' -> refactor(legion); Default -> feat(legion)
issue: Keyword-substring dispatch without precedence tiebreak. 'Refactor the test for the doc bug fix' matches four types. 4.7 picks first-listed deterministically, mis-classifying many reasonable tasks. Same defect class as S02c intent-teams cross-cut and LEGION-47-028 (learn.md).
remediation_sketch: Add explicit precedence rule: 'Evaluate in this order; first match wins: FIX_MODE -> fix; then test; then docs; then refactor; else feat.' Alternatively, AskUserQuestion when 2+ match.

excerpt: Use adapter.ask_user: 'Does this plan breakdown look right?' 'Looks good, generate the plans' -- proceed, 'Swap an agent' -- ask which plan, present alternatives, update, 'Adjust the plan structure' -- discuss changes, revise decomposition. Loop until user confirms
issue: Central user-facing gate of /legion:plan. Em-dash prose format, no closure. 'Swap an agent' branch has two nested free-text captures (inherits S03 adapter.prompt_free_text contract defect). 'Adjust the plan structure' says 'discuss changes' — unbounded free-text with no termination. P1 because failure here produces wrong waves/agents for the entire phase, amplified by --auto default behavior (LEGION-47-046).
remediation_sketch: Wrap outer in explicit AskUserQuestion with 3 items + closure. 'Swap an agent': follow-up AskUserQuestion enumerating plan IDs, then agent candidates. 'Adjust': AskUserQuestion with 'Change wave structure'/'Change plan boundaries'/'Change task breakdown'/'Done adjusting'. Cross-reference S03 adapter.prompt_free_text decision.

excerpt: AUTO-PIPELINE MODE: If $ARGUMENTS contains --auto: Skip all user confirmation gates (steps 3.5b, 3.6b, 6, 8.5) — auto-select recommended defaults. Run the full pipeline without pausing: board quick-assess → decomposition → plan critique → design review → security scan. If any stage raises a BLOCKER-severity escalation: halt and present to user
issue: 'Recommended defaults' not operationally defined for Step 6 plan-confirmation gate. Cascade lacks per-stage abort criteria beyond BLOCKER escalation. CAUTION/WARNING mid-pipeline behavior unspecified.
remediation_sketch: Enumerate defaults per gate explicitly. Add per-stage abort predicate for CAUTION/REWORK. Document which stages run on which severity levels.

excerpt: L88-92 'Re-plan from scratch'/'Keep existing plans'; L182-188 'Yes, generate 2-3 proposals'/'Skip'; L215-218 'Use existing'/'Regenerate'; L222-228 'Yes, create spec first'/'No, proceed to planning (Recommended)'; L285-291 'Run plan critique'/'Skip critique'
issue: Five parallel adapter.ask_user gates with em-dash/hyphen prose formatting, no closure. Several embed '(Recommended)' inside option text — 4.7 may include parenthetical when matching returned value. L88 is destructive (deletes plans). Repeat of S03/S04 closed-set-enforcement pattern.
remediation_sketch: Convert all five to explicit AskUserQuestion with structured label/description pairs. Move '(Recommended)' to description field. Document shared closure clause in CLAUDE.md. For L88 destructive prompt, require confirmation sub-question before deletion.

excerpt: MARKETING PHASE DETECTION: Check if phase description contains marketing keywords ('campaign','content calendar','social media','cross-channel','marketing','brand awareness','audience','engagement strategy'). DESIGN PHASE DETECTION: Check if phase description contains design keywords ('design system','component library','UX research',...)
issue: Inlined keyword lists; per S02c cross-cut, intent-teams.yaml has no marketing/design keyword registry, so plan.md becomes de-facto registry with drift risk vs. marketing-workflows/design-workflows skills. 'Contains' not operationalized (case? word boundary? stopword?). 'audience' matches non-marketing phases.
remediation_sketch: Move keyword lists to intent-teams.yaml. Replace inline with config reference. Operationalize match: case-insensitive word-boundary, exact phrase for multi-word keywords. Add multi-domain handling for phases matching both registries.

excerpt: AUTO-PIPELINE MODE ... If any stage raises a BLOCKER-severity escalation: halt the pipeline. AUTO-REFINE MODE ... If still REWORK after 2 cycles: halt and present to user
issue: --auto and --auto-refine delegate authority to the command itself; skip gates without referencing CLAUDE.md Authority Matrix or escalation-protocol.yaml. Some auto-selected decisions (architecture with new dependencies, spec with API changes) require human approval per Authority Matrix. --auto could silently commit those.
remediation_sketch: Add clause: auto modes must not skip gates where auto-selected default triggers an Authority Matrix escalation type (architecture, dependency, schema, api, deletion). Emit <escalation> block and halt regardless of --auto. Reference authority matrix explicitly.

excerpt: CAUTION: If AUTO_REFINE=false: user chooses to apply mitigations or proceed (existing behavior). REWORK: If AUTO_REFINE=false: user chooses to revise plans or proceed anyway (existing behavior)
issue: Two references to 'existing behavior' without stating what it is. 4.7 reading plan.md in isolation (without plan-critique SKILL.md loaded) cannot determine flow. No adapter.ask_user wrapper, no option enumeration, no done-state for the non-auto path.
remediation_sketch: Replace 'existing behavior' with explicit pointers to plan-critique Section 3 Step 4.b (CAUTION gate) and 4.c (REWORK gate). Inline option list as backstop for 4.7 reading without skill loaded.

excerpt: Present selected reviewers to user via adapter.ask_user; options include '{primary_agent_name} + {secondary_agent_name}' (Recommended), '{primary_agent_name} only', '{alternative_agent_name} + {primary_agent_name}', and 'Other' (accept custom agent IDs from user input)
issue: Primary reviewer-selection gate. 'Other' option delegates to free-text capture — inherits the S03 adapter.prompt_free_text contract defect (parallel to LEGION-47-041 quick.md L81-93 and LEGION-47-045 plan.md L263). {alternative_agent_name} template placeholder may render duplicate or unresolved. Em-dash descriptions inline in option labels. No closure framing. P1 because reviewer selection determines what findings the review produces; wrong-agent selection either misses defects or over-reports. Load-bearing decision gate.
remediation_sketch: Remove 'Other' from this AskUserQuestion; offer 'Pick different reviewers' which opens chained AskUserQuestion enumerating review-capable agent IDs from agent-registry (paginated by division). Guard the third option: if alternative_agent_name is null, drop it. Move em-dash descriptions into AskUserQuestion description field. Add closure. Cross-reference S03 adapter.prompt_free_text decision before full remediation.

excerpt: For each must-fix finding, determine the fix agent by file type: .md skill/command/agent/planning files -> autonomous; .ts/.js/.jsx/.tsx -> engineering-frontend-developer or engineering-backend-architect; .py/.rb/.go/.rs -> engineering-backend-architect; .css/.scss/design assets -> design-ux-architect; marketing/content .md -> marketing-content-creator; CI/CD/infrastructure configs -> engineering-devops-automator; No clear match -> autonomous
issue: Fix-agent routing table with multiple structural defects. (1) Classifier ambiguity: '.md skill/command/agent/planning files' has no operational predicate. (2) '.ts/.js/.jsx/.tsx -> frontend OR backend' has no tiebreak. (3) Two agent IDs do not exist in the Legion roster: engineering-devops-automator (correct: engineering-infrastructure-devops) and marketing-content-creator (correct: marketing-content-social-strategist). 4.7 dispatching to non-existent agents triggers AGENTS_DIR file-not-found or silently falls through to autonomous. (4) 'marketing/content .md' and 'CI/CD/infrastructure configs' have no path/extension predicates. P1 because this routing table is the only dispatch spec for fix cycles. Pre-existing issue unrelated to 4.7 literalism: the non-existent agent IDs will break the review flow regardless of reader.
remediation_sketch: Replace each row with path-glob + fallback (skills/**/*.md -> autonomous; commands/**/*.md -> autonomous). For .ts/.js: disambiguate by path. Correct agent IDs against CLAUDE.md Division table. Concrete path globs for marketing (content/**/*.md, campaigns/**/*.md) and CI/CD (.github/**, Dockerfile*, docker-compose*.yml). Flag non-existent agent IDs as pre-existing bug for separate remediation.

excerpt: Three user-facing ask_user gates: NL routing HIGH/MEDIUM/LOW options; review mode 'Dynamic review panel (Recommended)' / 'Classic reviewer selection'; escalation 'Fix manually' / 'Accept as-is' / 'Investigate further'
issue: Three recurring defects: em-dash descriptions inline, no closure framing, and in the LOW-confidence NL branch (L114-115) the option list is dynamically constructed from result.fallbackSuggestion without bounds — 'pick a suggestion or proceed with standard review' is a prose sketch, not an enumerable option list. The L626 escalation gate is destructive-adjacent ('Accept as-is and move to /legion:plan {N+1}' marks a phase complete despite unresolved blockers).
remediation_sketch: Wrap all three in explicit AskUserQuestion with structured label/description pairs and closure. For LOW branch: require fallback suggestions to be enumerated as exactly N options (cap at 5); if fewer, drop the branch and default to standard review. For L626: add confirmation sub-question before Accept-as-is commits phase-complete state.

excerpt: Step 5 loop exit: (e) if PASS (must-fix empty AND all reviewers PASS) -> Path A; (f) if NEEDS WORK or FAIL and cycle_count < max_cycles -> re-review; (g) if cycle_count >= max_cycles AND blockers remain -> Path B (escalation)
issue: Three exit conditions do not partition the state space. Edge: must-fix list EMPTY but at least one reviewer returned NEEDS WORK for style-only SUGGESTIONs. 5.e fails (not all PASS). 5.f triggers on NEEDS WORK but re-review has nothing to fix, loops identically. 5.g fails (no blockers). Loop runs to max_cycles with no progress, exits implicitly at L436 LOOP END. Path B only reachable if blockers remain; WARNINGs-only at max_cycles reaches neither Path A nor Path B.
remediation_sketch: Rewrite exit conditions to partition state: at end of each cycle, if must-fix empty -> Path A (PASS); elif cycle_count >= max_cycles -> Path B (ESCALATED with remaining must-fix); else fix cycle and re-review. Decouple pass verdict from individual reviewer strings; aggregate must-fix list is authoritative. NEEDS WORK without a must-fix finding = treat as SUGGESTION only.

excerpt: 6. COMPLETE REVIEW — Determine outcome based on loop result: If REVIEW_MODE === 'full': [Existing Step 6 logic unchanged]
issue: '[Existing Step 6 logic unchanged]' is merge-conflict marker or editor stub, not operational instruction. Actual Path A/B content begins at L512. 4.7-literal reader encountering L446 will skip entire Step 6 body or treat bracket note as instruction. Intent is 'fall through to Path A/B below' but not stated. Same defect class as LEGION-47-050 (plan.md 'existing behavior' references).
remediation_sketch: Delete the bracketed stub. Replace with: 'If REVIEW_MODE === full: proceed to Path A (passed) or Path B (escalated) below based on loop exit condition (Step 5.e or 5.g).'

excerpt: DETECT MANUAL EDITS: Run: git diff --name-only HEAD (shows uncommitted changes since the last commit). Intersect files in BOTH files_modified AND git diff output. If intersection non-empty: Report manual edit(s).
issue: Precondition is wrong. git diff --name-only HEAD shows uncommitted working-tree changes vs HEAD. Build command commits its output per Legion convention; post-build working tree is clean. User manual-edit then commit (e.g., via another Legion auto-commit) would NOT appear in git diff HEAD. Detection window is 'uncommitted-at-review-time', not 'all manual edits since build'. Narrower window probably intentional but undocumented; 'since the last commit' misleadingly suggests broader window.
remediation_sketch: Replace with: git log --format=%H {build-commit-sha}..HEAD -- {files_modified} to list commits after build commit that touched build-modified files. Manual edits = commits in range whose message does not match ^(feat|fix|chore|docs|test|refactor)\(legion\):. Additionally git diff HEAD -- {files_modified} catches uncommitted edits. Document build-commit-SHA retrieval from SUMMARY.md or phase-directory git log.

excerpt: security-review: only if --just-security flag is set, or security-sensitive files detected in SUMMARY.md (auth/crypto/permission/token/session files)
issue: 'Security-sensitive files detected' operationalized by substring-match against auth/crypto/permission/token/session keywords. No word-boundary rule — authorized_users_export.md and token_generator_template.md match but are not security-sensitive; passwd.go would not match but is. No case rule. No path-vs-filename distinction. Recurring keyword-substring-match defect class (peers: LEGION-47-028 learn.md, LEGION-47-044 quick.md, LEGION-47-048 plan.md).
remediation_sketch: Replace with precise detection: read each path in SUMMARY.md files_modified. Mark as security-sensitive if (a) any path component matches ^(auth|crypto|security|oauth|jwt|session|secret|credential) case-insensitive, OR (b) filename matches (auth|login|password|token|key|cert|tls|ssl)[-_.]. Enumerate explicitly; reject substring match.

excerpt: 6. ASK USER — Present via adapter.ask_user: 'Ship readiness confirmed. All gates passed. Proceed?' If github_available=true: Create PR / Push without PR / Abort — review needed. If github_available=false: Push to remote / Mark as shipped / Abort — review needed.
issue: Two bounded-option ask_user gates at the single most destructive commit point in the Legion lifecycle. Neither branch declares 'these are the only valid choices.' 4.7 may invent a fourth option (e.g., Squash-merge and tag release) that appears reasonable but has never been validated. The stem 'Proceed?' is a yes/no question grafted onto a 3-option menu — admits no natural mapping to the options. Same failure class as LEGION-47-041, 045, 051, 053. Elevated to P1 because this is the irreversible publish boundary.
remediation_sketch: Wrap each branch in its own explicit AskUserQuestion invocation. Rewrite stem to match multiple-choice surface: 'How do you want to publish Phase {N}?' Add closure: 'Choose exactly one. Do not propose alternatives. Do not combine options.' Enumerate exhaustive option set inline in prompt body.

excerpt: If any check fails: ... Present via adapter.ask_user: 'Canary detected a regression. What would you like to do?' Options: Investigate / Rollback / Continue monitoring
issue: Third bounded-option gate in this file. Under alert pressure (regression detected in production), open-endedness of 'What would you like to do?' invites 4.7 to propose unvalidated recovery actions (e.g., 'Disable the failing feature flag', 'Restart the service') that are not in the ship-pipeline repertoire. Same defect pattern as LEGION-47-058; distinct surface (alert-response taxonomy).
remediation_sketch: Rewrite as AskUserQuestion with explicit closure and option labels typed to ship-pipeline rollback vocabulary: 'Choose one of exactly three responses: A Investigate, B Rollback (print git revert — execution manual), C Continue monitoring. Do not propose other recovery actions.'

excerpt: 3. PRE-SHIP GATE — Run ship-pipeline Section 1 quality gate checks. Each check must pass before proceeding: a. Build completeness b. Review status c. Escalation check d. Verification commands e. Tests pass f. Clean working tree
issue: Gate enumerates 6 checks but does not specify (1) ordering, (2) short-circuit vs exhaustive behavior ('Each check must pass before proceeding' is ambiguous — proceeding to next check or past gate?), (3) fan-out for 3.d verification_commands. 4.7 may stop on first fail and never show full failure set — but summary table 'Result: {all_passed} of 6 gates passed' requires exhaustive evaluation. Consumer of ship-pipeline Section 1 semantics.
remediation_sketch: State explicitly: run all 6 in listed order, accumulate failures into gate_failures list, do not short-circuit. For 3.d run verification commands sequentially (build caches collide in parallel). After all checks if gate_failures non-empty display summary + per-failure remediation then exit.

excerpt: 4. GENERATE SHIP REPORT ... ## Files Modified {aggregated file list from all SUMMARY.md files, deduplicated} ... ## Escalation Status {list of escalations and their resolutions, or 'No escalations'}
issue: Ship report template has 7 sections but only Escalation Status specifies empty-state rendering. Under 4.7 literalism: if a phase has zero files modified (docs-only), should Files Modified heading render empty, render '(none)', or be omitted? Template shape implies mandatory rendering but empty heading looks broken in PR body. No report-level done predicate.
remediation_sketch: Add explicit empty-state rules per section: 'If list empty, render [specified string]. Do not omit heading.' Add report-level predicate: 'Report complete when all 7 sections rendered with content OR specified empty-state string. Never render empty heading with no body.'

excerpt: If 'Rollback': - Display the git revert command but do NOT execute automatically - Display: 'Run `git revert {commit_hash}` to roll back. Automatic rollback disabled for safety.'
issue: {commit_hash} is a placeholder with no stated source. 4.7 does not know which commit to revert: PR merge commit, branch-push commit, state-update commit from Step 7e (wrong), or original feature commits (multiple). Silent ambiguity: agent will display literal {commit_hash} or guess HEAD, both wrong. Precondition: command needs to capture and persist the ship-commit SHA at push/PR moment.
remediation_sketch: Add substep to Step 5 Path A/B: after push, capture SHIP_COMMIT_SHA = git rev-parse HEAD (Path A: merge commit if auto-merged). Persist to STATE.md under Phase {N} ship_commit_sha. In rollback: display `git revert {SHIP_COMMIT_SHA}` read from STATE.md. If no SHA (Path C): 'No ship commit recorded. Manual rollback requires identifying commit range.'

excerpt: e. Tests pass: Run test command from adapter or project config - Check PROJECT.md or package.json/Makefile for test command - Run the test suite - If tests fail: 'GATE FAIL: Test suite failed. Fix failing tests before shipping.'
issue: 'Check PROJECT.md or package.json/Makefile' leaves precedence order, search procedure, fallback unspecified. 4.7 may pick any of three arbitrarily, run none if PROJECT.md has no explicit field, or invoke npm test when Makefile is authoritative. 'Fix failing tests' response shape unbounded — no pointer to which test, what file, which stderr line.
remediation_sketch: Replace with explicit precedence chain: stop at first hit — (1) PROJECT.md ## Test Command fenced block; (2) package.json scripts.test; (3) Makefile test target; (4) pyproject.toml tool.pytest; (5) go.mod present. If none: tests_check=SKIP. On failure display {command} (exit {code}) and last 20 lines of stderr — show raw output.

excerpt: 1. PRE-FLIGHT CHECK ... If it exists: use adapter.ask_user to confirm reinitialize - 'A project already exists in .planning/. Reinitialize from scratch?' Option 1: 'Yes, start fresh' - continue (will overwrite PROJECT.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md). Option 2: 'No, keep existing' - abort and suggest /legion:status instead
issue: Destructive 2-option gate (yes = overwrite three state files) without closure framing. 4.7 may fabricate 'Archive existing, start fresh' or 'Migrate only STATE.md' — both sound reasonable but have no implementation, leaving inconsistent state. Stem 'Reinitialize from scratch?' is yes/no framed but option labels are full phrases — mismatch invites interpolation. Same class as LEGION-47-051/053/058. P1 because this is a destructive gate running before any state is captured.
remediation_sketch: Wrap in explicit AskUserQuestion. Rewrite stem: 'A project already exists in .planning/. Choose exactly one of two actions.' Option 1 'Overwrite existing project with fresh start (irreversible — PROJECT.md, ROADMAP.md, STATE.md replaced).' Option 2 'Keep existing project and abort /legion:start (no files modified).' Closure: 'Do not propose a third option. Do not merge or archive.'

excerpt: 2. EXPLORATION OFFER ... 'Before we create your project plan, would you like to explore and clarify your idea first?' Options: **'Yes, explore with Polymath'** (Recommended for new ideas) ... After exploration completes and user selects 'Proceed to planning' ... 'Explore more' or 'Park' ... **'No, jump straight to planning'** (Best if you already have a clear concept) ... Default selection: 'Yes, explore with Polymath' (first option)
issue: Nested bounded-option gate with four problems: (1) embeds a second 3-option set from /legion:explore ('Proceed to planning'/'Explore more'/'Park') inline — 4.7 may treat them as first-level choices; (2) 'Default selection' ambiguous — pre-selected? auto-timeout? recommendation? AskUserQuestion has no default notion and auto-selecting silently violates CLAUDE.md; (3) 'Recommended for new ideas'/'Best if you already have a clear concept' invite 4.7 to answer on user's behalf; (4) yes/no stem mismatches full-phrase option labels. First user-facing gate of fresh project. P1.
remediation_sketch: Wrap in AskUserQuestion. Rewrite stem: 'Choose exactly one of two planning entry paths.' Remove 'Default selection' — unsupported and violates CLAUDE.md. Move explore-mode exit options out of this prompt — they are /legion:explore exit states, not options here. Closure: 'Do not pre-select. Do not propose exploration with a different agent.'

excerpt: If existing source code detected: Use adapter.ask_user: 'I see an existing codebase here. Should I map it before we plan?' Option 1: 'Yes, analyze the codebase first' ... Option 2: 'No, skip the analysis' ... Option 3: 'I'll run /legion:plan directly' -> Abort start, let user plan manually
issue: 3-option gate where Option 3 is not an analysis choice but cancel-and-redirect. Type confusion: Options 1-2 answer 'should I map it', Option 3 answers 'should /legion:start be running at all?'. 4.7 may merge Options 2 and 3 semantically (both skip mapping) or fabricate 'Map only the src/ directory'. Also 'greenfield mode' at L77 is undefined — precondition violation layered on closed-set problem.
remediation_sketch: Split into two AskUserQuestions. First (exit-gate): 'Continue with /legion:start, or exit and run /legion:plan directly? A Continue B Exit.' Second (only if A): 'Map codebase before planning? A Yes analyze (run codebase-mapper Sections 2-4, write .planning/CODEBASE.md). B No skip (plan without CODEBASE.md).' Define greenfield mode inline.

excerpt: 7. QUESTIONING STAGE 3: WORKFLOW PREFERENCES ... Use adapter.ask_user with 3 structured choice questions: - Execution mode: Guided (Recommended) / Autonomous / Collaborative - Planning depth: Standard (Recommended) / Quick Sketch / Deep Analysis - Cost profile: Balanced (Recommended) / Economy / Premium
issue: Three 3-option gates rendered inline as bullets. No closure, no stem, no definitions. (a) 'Guided/Autonomous/Collaborative' undefined in this file — 4.7 may infer wrong semantics or invent hybrid; (b) '(Recommended)' has no implementation rule — pre-selected? fallback? advisory?; (c) three questions not wrapped in three separate AskUserQuestion invocations — 4.7 may render as single multi-select (invalid in Claude Code) or skip by assuming recommended defaults. CLAUDE.md mandates AskUserQuestion for each.
remediation_sketch: Replace bullets with three explicit AskUserQuestion specs, each with stem, 3 enumerated options with definitions, and closure. E.g., 'Execution mode — exactly one: A Guided (pauses at each phase gate); B Autonomous (runs to completion); C Collaborative (pauses at each plan).' Remove '(Recommended)' unless AskUserQuestion supports preselect hint.

excerpt: 3. BROWNFIELD DETECTION ... Check for non-Legion source files: - Any source files outside .planning/, .claude/, .codex/, .cursor/, .windsurf/, .gemini/, .opencode/, and .aider/? - Any package.json, Gemfile, pyproject.toml, requirements.txt, go.mod at root? - Any src/, app/, lib/, components/ directories?
issue: Triple-heuristic trigger without aggregation rule. Do all three must match, any one, or weighted score? 'Source files' undefined. Excluded-dir list omits .kiro/ (Kiro runtime, per AGENTS.md) and .legion/ (fallback install, per update.md L41). L66 'src/, app/, lib/, components/' silently excludes internal/, cmd/, pkg/ (Go), tests/, spec/ (Ruby) — but codebase-mapper may match them, creating behavioral drift. Peer of LEGION-47-002, 004, 048.
remediation_sketch: Replace with delegation: 'Run codebase-mapper skill Section 1 Source Code Detection Heuristic. If detected: true proceed with brownfield; if false skip. Do not duplicate inline — codebase-mapper is authoritative.' If delegation not feasible, enumerate exhaustively: extension list, directory-match rule, case rule, aggregation (logical OR across bullets).

excerpt: First: Check for saved exploration files (even if explore wasn't run this session) - Look for .planning/exploration-*.md files - If found: read the most recent one (by filename or modification time) - Extract: Crystallized Summary, Knowns, Unknowns, Decisions Made - Set exploration_context = extracted data
issue: Two underspecified preconditions. (1) 'by filename or modification time' — which? L53 writes exploration-{timestamp}.md but timestamp format unspecified (ISO8601? unix? date?). Non-deterministic if formats differ across sessions. (2) 'Extract: Crystallized Summary, Knowns, Unknowns, Decisions Made' — these are section titles but exploration file schema not specified here or in <context>. If /legion:explore produces different schema, extraction silently fails and exploration_context is partial. Peer of LEGION-47-042, 056.
remediation_sketch: Specify timestamp format: 'Files named .planning/exploration-YYYY-MM-DDTHH-MM-SSZ.md (ISO8601 UTC, colons replaced with hyphens). Sort alphabetically descending = mtime descending when format honored.' Add section-presence check: 'Verify file contains exactly these H2 headings: ## Crystallized Summary, ## Knowns, ## Unknowns, ## Decisions Made. If any missing: exploration_context = null and log schema mismatch.'

excerpt: <objective> Guide the user through an adaptive questioning flow to capture project vision, requirements, and constraints. Produce PROJECT.md, ROADMAP.md, and STATE.md with recommended agents per phase. </objective> <process> 1. PRE-FLIGHT CHECK
issue: Objective describes outputs (three files) but omits acceptance predicate tying the 12-step process together. No 'command is done when ...' statement. Under 4.7 literalism a step may silently no-op if loading fails (Step 3 ambiguous heuristic; Step 5-7 questioning off-rails on one-word answers). Steps don't state 'cannot proceed to N+1 until N produces artifact X.' Peer of LEGION-47-013 (plan.md intent-front-loading).
remediation_sketch: Add to <objective>: 'DONE WHEN all three of: (a) PROJECT.md exists with non-empty Requirements section; (b) ROADMAP.md exists with at least 1 phase and each phase has 2-4 recommended agents; (c) STATE.md exists and references Phase 1 as current. If any step raises a blocking escalation, stop before Step 12 — do not write partial state.' Also add one-line purpose at top of each numbered step.

excerpt: 5. DETERMINE NEXT ACTION — Apply this decision tree in order (first match wins): a. No ROADMAP.md exists ... b. Current phase status contains 'pending' or 'Pending' ... c. contains 'Planned' ... d. contains 'Executed' or STATE.md says 'pending review' ... e. 'Complete' AND more phases ... e2. 'Complete' AND last phase of milestone AND all phases Complete (takes priority over e) ... f. All phases 'Complete' ... g. STATE.md says 'escalated' or 'failed'
issue: First-match-wins decision tree with three closed-set leaks. (1) Branch e2 injected out of order with rule 'takes priority over e when milestone boundary reached' overrides first-match-wins — two match-selection rules collide, 4.7 cannot resolve. (2) Branches b-d use 'contains X' — case-insensitive implied not stated, substring means 'pending review' hits b and d simultaneously (first match → always b, dropping L253 review suggestion). (3) Status values enumerated here ('pending','Planned','Executed','Complete','Shipped','escalated','failed') mismatch validate.md L64 canonical 6-value set.
remediation_sketch: (a) Fix ordering: rename e2 to e, demote milestone-less branch to f, eliminating priority conflict. (b) Replace 'contains X' with '== X' case-sensitive against canonical value set from validate.md. (c) Add '== pending review' as distinct status from STATE.md Status: field. (d) Add fall-through branch h: report status and fields, suggest manual diagnosis. Do not guess.

excerpt: 2. READ PROJECT STATE ... b. .planning/STATE.md — extract: - Current phase number and total phases (from 'Phase: N of M') - Phase status (from 'Status:' field) - Last activity (from 'Last Activity:' field) - Next action (from 'Next Action:' field) - Recent decisions (from 'Recent Decisions' section, last 3) ...
issue: STATE.md field extraction uses hand-written label patterns as triggers without specifying (1) exact regex, (2) case rule, (3) whitespace tolerance, (4) fallback when template variant renders label differently. Peer of LEGION-47-044/048 (substring-keyword trigger class). state-template.md authoritative schema referenced from start.md L22, not here. A v7.3 STATE.md rendering 'Phase (N of M)' with parens silently degrades.
remediation_sketch: Delegate to execution-tracker or new state-parser skill with explicit schema: 'Parse STATE.md by state-schema-v1. Canonical field names, label formats, line positions in skills/execution-tracker/state-schema.md. On missing required field surface diagnostic warning in dashboard — do not silently omit. Do not infer or guess.' Or enumerate regex inline with explicit case/whitespace rules.

excerpt: 5b. CONTEXT-AWARE SUGGESTIONS ... Uses intent-router Section 8: getContextSuggestions(). a. Call `getContextSuggestions('.planning/STATE.md')` from intent-router Section 8 b. Receive current lifecycle position and 2-3 ranked suggestions ... If no suggestions available (degraded state): 'Run /legion:status for orientation or /legion:start to begin a new project.'
issue: Function-call precondition unverified. (1) 'intent-router Section 8' exists only if skills/intent-router/SKILL.md loaded — execution_context L15-20 lists as always-loaded but Step 0 L40-47 conditional skill loading does NOT include intent-router. Discrepancy. (2) getContextSuggestions() is symbolic — 4.7 must simulate by reading Section 8 prose; if missing/renamed behavior undefined. (3) Degradation at L300-304 says skip silently but L282 heading expected to render — conflict between skip and render fallback.
remediation_sketch: Add precondition at top of Step 5b: 'Verify skills/intent-router/SKILL.md is loaded and contains Section 8 with getContextSuggestions subsection specifying input/output schema. If missing: skip Step 5b entirely (no heading). If loaded: invoke per schema.' Resolve degradation conflict: 'On any degradation omit ## Suggested Next Actions heading entirely. Do not render fallback message — rest of dashboard provides orientation.'

excerpt: <process> DRY-RUN MODE (deterministic, no side effects) - If $ARGUMENTS contains --dry-run, DO NOT write files ... - Stop after reporting. 0. CONDITIONAL SKILL LOADING (context budget) ... 1. CHECK PROJECT EXISTS
issue: Step-numbering and intent-ordering defects. (1) DRY-RUN MODE at L29 has no numeric prefix. L40 jumps to '0.' L48 to '1.'. 4.7 may interpret as 'do DRY-RUN first always' (partial) or treat unnumbered block as scaffolding and skip to step 0. (2) Command's DONE state spread across L8-13 <objective> and Step 6 DISPLAY NEXT ACTION — no upfront 'done when dashboard displayed AND next-action printed.' Peer of LEGION-47-070, 013.
remediation_sketch: (a) Renumber: dry-run handling = Step 0 (currently unnumbered), conditional skill loading = Step 0.5 or folded into Step 0, re-index downstream. (b) Add to <objective>: 'DONE WHEN both (a) dashboard block (Step 4) rendered; (b) next-action line matching one of branches a-g OR fall-through diagnostic printed. If Step 2 cannot read PROJECT.md, stop at Step 1 with No Legion project found message.'

excerpt: - If $ARGUMENTS contains --dry-run, DO NOT write files, spawn agents, commit, or perform external side effects. ... Omit this section entirely. Do NOT show a placeholder, suggestion, or 'no memory' message. ... If .planning/board/ does not exist or contains no meeting directories: Omit this section entirely. Do NOT show a placeholder. ... If github_metadata_available is false: Omit this section entirely. Do NOT show a placeholder.
issue: Four 'DO NOT'/'Do NOT' prohibitions cluster rendering rules. Three omit-section rules at L185, L196-197, L219 identical in intent — could be stated once positively. 4.7 may interpret 'Omit ... Do NOT show a placeholder' as two distinct actions (omit heading AND avoid emitting separate placeholder message elsewhere) — double negation risks defensive rendering of empty heading.
remediation_sketch: Add top-of-file rendering rule: 'Section rendering policy — render section only if include predicate true. If false: omit entire section including H2 heading. Never render empty section, placeholder, or no-X-available message. Predicates listed per section.' Then simplify sites: L185 → include: memory_available && record_count > 0; L196 → board_dir_exists && meeting_count > 0; L219 → github_metadata_available.

excerpt: - Workflow: {mode}, {depth}, {cost_profile}
issue: cost_profile is a template variable in the questioning summary but no settings key, schema field, or skill consumes it. settings.schema.json defines models.{planning,execution,check} and planning_reasoning — no cost_profile. Grep across skills/, commands/, adapters/, .planning/config/ yields zero readers. Likely an artifact of an earlier design where cost tier was an explicit user choice that got refactored into adapter mappings without removing the reference.
remediation_sketch: Path A: delete the {cost_profile} reference from start.md:202 and any companion question in skills/questioning-flow/. Path B: implement cost_profile as a real settings key (models.cost_profile: premium|balanced|budget) mapping to fixed {planning,execution,check} triplets. Recommend path A — current models.{planning,execution,check} already handles per-tier control.

excerpt: 4. COMPARE VERSIONS - If INSTALLED_VERSION == LATEST_VERSION: Display: 'Legion is up to date (v{INSTALLED_VERSION}).' Stop. - Display: 'Update available: v{INSTALLED_VERSION} -> v{LATEST_VERSION}'
issue: String equality on version strings is silently wrong for semver. Lexicographic: '1.0.10' < '1.0.2', '2.0.0' > '10.0.0'. 4.7 implements == as string compare (most literal reading), 'up to date' branch fires for wrong pairs in routine upgrades. No explicit ordering check — if INSTALLED > LATEST (dev build ahead of npm), falls through to 'Update available' prompting downgrade. No criterion 'only prompt when LATEST strictly newer per semver precedence.'
remediation_sketch: Explicit semver compare: (a) if semver-equal → 'up to date' and stop; (b) if INSTALLED > LATEST → 'Legion is ahead of npm (local v{X}, npm v{Y}). No update available.' and stop — do not prompt downgrade; (c) if INSTALLED < LATEST → 'Update available' and proceed. Use npx semver or parse strings numerically per field — do not use string equality.

excerpt: 5. CONFIRM AND INSTALL - Use adapter.ask_user to confirm: 'Update Legion from v{INSTALLED_VERSION} to v{LATEST_VERSION}?' - Option 1: 'Yes, update now' - Option 2: 'No, skip this update' - If user confirms: Run: Bash npx @9thlevelsoftware/legion@latest {runtime_flag} --{INSTALL_SCOPE} Display the installer output - Remind user to restart their CLI to pick up updated commands
issue: 2-option confirmation gate for destructive side effect (npx install overwrites Legion installation). Yes/no framing less risky than multi-option but still lacks closure. 4.7 may interpolate 'Yes but only the commands not the agents' or 'Download without installing' — unsupported. L84 'Remind user to restart their CLI' is sibling of both options (not nested under 'If user confirms') — literal reading prints restart reminder even on 'No, skip this update.'
remediation_sketch: Wrap in AskUserQuestion. Stem: 'Update Legion from v{X} to v{Y}? Choose exactly one of two actions.' Option A: 'Update now — run installer (overwrites current installation).' Option B: 'Skip this update — no changes.' Closure: 'Do not propose partial updates. Do not propose alternate install sources.' Move L84 inside Option A branch — it's an install-completion message, not universal suffix.

excerpt: 2. READ INSTALLED VERSION - Determine install scope and manifest location: - First check for a local install in the current project: - Claude Code: .claude/legion/manifest.json - All others: .legion/manifest.json - If no local manifest exists, fall back to the global install path: ... Store the winning scope as INSTALL_SCOPE (`local` or `global`) ... Parse the JSON and extract the 'version' field
issue: Three underspecified preconditions. (1) 'All others: .legion/manifest.json' — update.md contract but start.md L65 and codebase-mapper exclude .legion/ from brownfield detection with no mention — drift risk. (2) No JSON validation — if manifest valid JSON but no 'version' field or version null/non-string, 4.7 proceeds with INSTALLED_VERSION=null, cascading to L60 string compare triggering unnecessary update. (3) Concurrent-install ambiguity: both local .claude/legion/manifest.json AND global ~/.claude/legion/manifest.json with different versions — only local read, update runs --local, global drifts silently.
remediation_sketch: (1) State canonical install layout explicitly: .legion/ (all-runtimes local), ~/.legion/ (all-runtimes global), .claude/legion/ (claude-code local override), ~/.claude/legion/ (claude-code global override). (2) Validate parse: verify version field exists and is non-empty string. If missing/invalid: 'Manifest at {path} malformed. Re-install via npx.' and stop. (3) Detect concurrent installs: check other scope; if both exist with different versions warn and ask user which scope to update.

excerpt: 4.7. DISPLAY CHANGELOG (if update available) - Run: Bash npm show @9thlevelsoftware/legion --json 2>/dev/null - Extract the 'description' field for a quick summary - If the package has a 'homepage' field: display link 'Full changelog: {homepage}' - Display: 'What's new in v{LATEST_VERSION}: {description or See changelog for details}'
issue: Two response-shape defects. (1) npm package.json 'description' is a package tagline (e.g., 'Multi-CLI plugin for orchestrating AI specialists'), not a changelog. Labeling 'What's new in v{X}: ...' misrepresents content — every version shows same tagline. 4.7 renders label literally, user sees 'What's new in v7.4.0: Multi-CLI plugin for orchestrating...' — misleading. (2) No size/truncation bound on description. Some descriptions 300+ chars; no wrap/truncate/elide rule.
remediation_sketch: Replace with version-specific changelog fetch: 'npm view @9thlevelsoftware/legion@{LATEST_VERSION} --json. If response has repository.url pointing to GitHub, fetch CHANGELOG.md main branch, extract section for v{LATEST_VERSION}. Display (truncated 10 lines). Otherwise: Changelog: see {homepage}/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md — do not label description as Whats new.' Alternatively relabel as 'Package description:' (accurate) and separately offer changelog link.

excerpt: 6. POST-INSTALL VERIFICATION - After install completes, verify the update was successful: - Re-read manifest.json and confirm version matches LATEST_VERSION - If version mismatch: warn 'Update may not have completed successfully. Installed: {actual}, Expected: {LATEST_VERSION}' - Run checksum verification if available: - If checksums.sha256 exists in the install directory: verify file integrity - If verification fails: warn 'Checksum verification failed. Consider reinstalling.'
issue: Post-install 'verification' surfaces warnings but never defines DONE. If version mismatch fires, is install successful or failed? Command proceeds to implicit completion with no explicit DONE/FAIL predicate. User sees 'warning' and may interpret advisory — but version mismatch after install is hard failure. ship.md has explicit GATE FAIL; this file lacks parallel gating. 'checksums.sha256 in install directory' — location not defined relative to INSTALL_SCOPE; if local scope but checksum only in global, check silently skips.
remediation_sketch: DONE predicate: 'Complete when (a) post-install re-read confirms manifest.version == LATEST_VERSION (exact string after parse), AND (b) if checksums.sha256 exists in {MANIFEST_PATH parent}, integrity check passes. If (a) fails: Update FAILED — installed v{X} does not match expected v{Y}. Re-run... and exit non-zero. If (b) fails: integrity failure and DO NOT claim success.' Define checksum path: {dirname MANIFEST_PATH}/checksums.sha256 — same scope as manifest.

excerpt: Check intent-teams.yaml: - If .planning/config/intent-teams.yaml exists: - Parse the YAML. For each intent under the `intents:` mapping: - Extract agent IDs from `agents.primary` list ... `agents.secondary` list ... `filter.exclude_agents` list ... Any missing -> record as FAIL with: 'intent-teams.yaml intent {intent_name} references agent {id} which does not exist in agents/'
issue: The parser contract does not match the actual schema of .planning/config/intent-teams.yaml. Per S02c cross-cutting observation, the file's top-level keys are command_routes:, natural_language:, context_rules: — there is no `intents:` mapping, no agents.primary/secondary, no filter.exclude_agents. A literal 4.7 reader iterates zero intents and silently PASSes (false negative), or throws a parse error not mapped in the Scoring block. Pre-existing bug analogous to LEGION-47-052 fix-agent routing table references.
remediation_sketch: Re-read the actual intent-teams.yaml schema and rewrite the parser with path expressions matching the real fields. Add PARSE_ERROR -> WARN rule to the Scoring block. Flag as pre-existing bug in REMEDIATION.md.

excerpt: Check: `agent` field value is a valid agent ID (exists in the agent catalog via agent-registry). ... Build the ground-truth agent set: - List all `.md` files in the `agents/` directory. ... Store as VALID_AGENT_IDS set.
issue: Two different ground-truth sources for the same concept (valid agent IDs) used in adjacent steps with no documented priority. Step 6 L96 uses 'the agent catalog via agent-registry'; step 7b L128-131 builds VALID_AGENT_IDS directly from the filesystem. If sources diverge, the two steps report inconsistent results. 4.7 cannot infer which is authoritative.
remediation_sketch: Unify the source: define VALID_AGENT_IDS at step 1 from the filesystem only, and reference it from step 6. Do not consult agent-registry for primary validation. If divergence is a concern, add a dedicated WARN check comparing the two sources.

excerpt: Check that each division comment header count (e.g., '# TESTING DIVISION (6 agents)') matches the actual agent count for that division.
issue: Parse rule specified by example only, not regex or structural contract. 4.7 has to infer whitespace normalization, case sensitivity, singular/plural handling, and format variants. Same keyword-or-pattern-substring defect class as LEGION-47-028/044/048/057/072.
remediation_sketch: Replace example with explicit regex: ^\s*#\s+([A-Za-z][A-Za-z ]*?)\s+DIVISION\s*\((\d+)\s+agents?\)\s*$ case-insensitive. Specify division-name normalization to lowercase before comparison.

excerpt: If FIX_MODE was active and fixes were applied: Append: ## Fixes Applied - {description of each fix applied} Then re-run validation on fixed files and show updated results.
issue: Re-run contract has no termination bound and no 'done' definition. Scope ambiguity (just fixed files vs. full pipeline?), recursion possibility if second run FAILs introduce more fixes, output format undefined (same table? two tables?).
remediation_sketch: Specify: re-run steps 3-9 exactly once against full tree. Render 'Before Fixes' and 'After Fixes' sub-tables. Detect regressions (PASS->FAIL) as WARN. Hard-cap at two iterations per invocation.

excerpt: Exit. Validate never modifies phase state (STATE.md, ROADMAP.md progress fields) unless --fix is used for formatting corrections only. Validate never changes phase status, plan assignments, or roadmap progression.
issue: Prohibitive cluster tail with two 'never' statements. The second 'never' is redundant given the first plus the FIX_MODE enumerations in steps 3-5. 4.7 may read the second 'never' as a stricter constraint and refuse legitimate normalization operations like L72 'wip -> In Progress'.
remediation_sketch: Rewrite positively: enumerate the allowed write surface from steps 3, 4, 8 FIX_MODE blocks; drop both 'never' sentences.

excerpt: ## CLI Detection and Adapter Loading ... Throughout skills and commands, adapter fields are referenced with dot notation
issue: File is labeled 'compatibility shim' (frontmatter line 7; body line 11 says 'New command execution should load workflow-common-core first') yet redeclares CLI Detection, Adapter Reference, User Interaction, Settings Resolution, Agent Path Resolution Protocol, and State File Locations in parallel with workflow-common-core. Divergent counts (52 vs 48 agents; 9-item vs 7-item settings list) create implicit-precondition failures. Reader has no rule for which is canonical.
remediation_sketch: (a) Reduce to pure pointer to workflow-common-core/domains/github/memory extensions; or (b) annotate divergent sections as 'CANONICAL: see workflow-common-core §X'; or (c) delete file and migrate references. Path (a) is lowest-risk.

excerpt: Marketing workflows ... if MKT-* requirements or marketing keywords ... Design workflows ... if DSN-* requirements or design keywords
issue: Third occurrence of S02c cross-cut: 'marketing keywords' and 'design keywords' are unenumerated triggers. intent-teams.yaml has no keyword registry (S02c finding). 4.7 must over-match or under-match. More load-bearing than root-level parity findings because this file is loaded by /legion:plan execution context.
remediation_sketch: Mirror LEGION-47-002: drop 'or keywords' and restrict trigger to MKT-*/DSN-* prefix, or reference a concrete keyword source file once one exists.

excerpt: All personality reads use {AGENTS_DIR}/{agent-id}.md — never bare agents/{agent-id}.md
issue: Prohibitive rule ('never bare agents/{agent-id}.md') conflicts with cli-dispatch Section 3 Part 1 which uses exactly that forbidden pattern. 4.7 reading both files faces a choice between two authoritative statements.
