Prohibitive framing likely to override the positive cli-dispatch instruction, causing either refusal or silent substitution.
remediation_sketch: (a) Fix cli-dispatch Section 3 to use {AGENTS_DIR}/{agent-id}.md (see LEGION-47-121 target), OR (b) relax this rule to 'Use {AGENTS_DIR}/{agent-id}.md as canonical; bare accepted in local-dev'. Path (a) is correct.

excerpt: DIVISIONS = [ ... Engineering 7 agents ... Marketing 8 agents ... Testing 7 agents ... Support 6 agents ... ] TOTAL_AGENTS = 52
issue: Three conflicting agent counts: sum = 51, TOTAL_AGENTS declared = 52, CLAUDE.md Dynamic Knowledge Index = 48. No rule for which is canonical. Downstream agents injected with TOTAL_AGENTS=52 will assume a larger roster than reality, leading to invented agent IDs (same defect class as LEGION-47-052 non-existent agent ID).
remediation_sketch: Replace hardcoded counts with filesystem lookup: 'Run: ls {AGENTS_DIR}/*.md | wc -l for authoritative count; divisions read from agent frontmatter.' Add /legion:validate check comparing declared counts to filesystem reality.

excerpt: When commands or skills reference adapter.ask_user ... you MUST use the AskUserQuestion tool ... This applies to ALL question prompts in ALL commands
issue: Inheriting S03 cross-cut. Canonical declaration of user-interaction contract in always-load core. 'ALL question prompts in ALL commands' is absolute; AskUserQuestion requires non-empty options. But many commands capture free-text (project vision, task prompts) with no bounded options. Readers forced to synthesize fake options or trigger silent tool-schema errors when options=[] is rejected. No adapter.prompt_free_text primitive exists.
remediation_sketch: Introduce adapter.prompt_free_text sibling primitive. Split rule into bounded-choice (AskUserQuestion) vs free-text (prompt_free_text). Document command split. Ship before next release.

excerpt: Mode Profile Resolution ... 1. Read .planning/config/control-modes.yaml 2. Look up profiles[control_mode] ... 4. Pass resolved profile to authority-enforcer and wave-executor integration points
issue: Three Implicit Preconditions: (1) downstream consumers (authority-enforcer, wave-executor) must accept the 5-flag schema but no contract verification; (2) key lookup case sensitivity unspecified; (3) partial profile (subset of 5 flags present) handling unspecified. 4.7 may silently pass malformed profiles.
remediation_sketch: Specify: key lookup is case-sensitive; partial profiles warn+fallback to hardcoded guarded; 5-flag schema is contract. Add precondition assert before pass.

excerpt: Context Budget Ceiling (Core) ... build: soft 180 KB, hard 225 KB ... Release checks enforce hard ceilings and print telemetry for every command.
issue: HOW specified (KB numbers) but no WHY or action. Soft vs hard undefined — does soft trigger warning, pruning, or abort? Other 11 commands from L105-122 table have no budget entries — no rule for their treatment. 'Release checks enforce' defers to unspecified external process.
remediation_sketch: Front-load goal: prevent context overflow. Soft triggers telemetry warning; hard causes abort. Unlisted commands inherit soft 120/hard 150. Define enforcement location.

excerpt: Activate only when phase requirements or task context indicates domain relevance.
issue: Entire activation trigger is 'when phase requirements or task context indicates domain relevance'. 'Indicates domain relevance' has no enumeration, no canonical keyword list, no fallback. All 4 rules total do not define what 'domain relevance' means operationally. Parity with S02c cross-cut — marketing/design keyword registry absent from intent-teams.yaml. 4.7 either always activates (skill is loaded) or never activates (unprovable).
remediation_sketch: Concrete rule: Activate when (a) phase has requirement ID matching ^(MKT|DSN)-\d+, OR (b) user passed --domain=marketing|design, OR (c) phase frontmatter domain:=marketing|design. Drop 'indicates domain relevance' entirely.

excerpt: Require both: gh auth status success and a valid git remote.
issue: Precondition gate is underspecified relative to parent workflow-common Sec 649-655 which requires three checks: 'which gh' AND 'gh auth status' AND GitHub-hosted remote. Readers loading only this extension miss the 'which gh' check and hostname verification — 'git remote get-url origin' passes for any remote including GitLab/Bitbucket. Silent failures with confusing errors follow.
remediation_sketch: Expand rule 2 to three-part check or reference github-sync Section 1 as canonical verifier instead of duplicating here.

excerpt: Always reflect GitHub updates in local state files when relevant.
issue: 'when relevant' is textbook CAT-2 ambiguous trigger per rubric. No enumeration of relevance criteria. Which state files? Which events qualify? 4.7 either updates on every call (noisy, corrupting) or never updates (defeats rule).
remediation_sketch: Explicit: After any GitHub write op (create/update/close issue/PR, close milestone), mirror result to .planning/STATE.md ## GitHub within same invocation. Read-only ops do not trigger updates.

excerpt: Missing memory files must not block command execution.
issue: Graceful-degradation contract collapsed to one rule without the canonical 4-step check pattern from parent workflow-common L606-611. No enumeration of the 4 memory files (OUTCOMES/PATTERNS/ERRORS/PREFERENCES) — reader may assume single file. 4.7 loading this extension without the parent may attempt ops before existence check, producing errors this rule is meant to prevent.
remediation_sketch: Expand rule 3 to explicit 4-step: check .planning/memory/{file}.md exists where file in {OUTCOMES,PATTERNS,ERRORS,PREFERENCES}; if exists use, if not skip silently with log, never raise. Reference parent canonical section.

excerpt: Record outcomes/preferences only when workflow explicitly calls for it.
issue: Circular trigger — 'record when told to record'. Parent workflow-common L590-604 enumerates 14 concrete memory integration points; this extension does not reference them. 4.7 defaults to either never record (safe interpretation of 'only when') or record at every opportunity.
remediation_sketch: Replace with: 'Record memory only at the integration points listed in workflow-common § Memory Integration Points (14 canonical points across build/review/plan/status). Do not record outside these.'

excerpt: For MULTIPLE plans in the wave: Issue ALL agent spawn calls simultaneously per adapter.coordinate_parallel ... Do NOT spawn agents one at a time for multi-plan waves
issue: Core dispatch authority. 'Issue ALL agent spawn calls simultaneously' does not specify the mechanism — Claude Code parallel execution requires multiple tool calls in the SAME LLM response, not sequential calls across turns. 4.7's documented regression is defaulting to fewer subagents; without explicit 'single response block' instruction, 4.7 reads 'simultaneously' as 'as fast as possible, one at a time' which is the observed failure. Parent finding cited by LEGION-47-023 (commands/build.md).
remediation_sketch: Rewrite Step 4: 'For multi-plan waves, issue N Agent tool calls within a SINGLE LLM response block — do not split across turns. Your response contains exactly N Agent tool invocations in one tool_use array. If you issue call 1 then wait before issuing call 2, STOP — you serialized the wave.' Add counter-example and post-dispatch verification.

excerpt: wave: A | B # Wave A or Wave B ... wave_role: build | analysis | execution | remediation
issue: Section 7 Two-Wave Plan Structure uses wave: as a STRING (A/B) while Section 2 Step 3 L174 declares wave: as integer. Schema contradiction for same frontmatter field. Two-Wave Activation rules L1584 are underspecified ('plans span multiple service/page groups OR include explicit analysis tasks' — no enumeration). 4.7 cannot determine when to switch int→str semantics; parallel dispatch breaks either way.
remediation_sketch: Pick one schema: (a) unify on integer waves (A=1, B=2, drop string) or (b) explicit two_wave: true frontmatter flag switches interpretation. Document auto-activation detection or require explicit opt-in. Cross-reference plan-frontmatter.schema.json.

excerpt: Two-wave pattern activates when: ... Plans span multiple service/page groups OR include explicit analysis tasks ... Standard single-wave execution when: ... All plans touch same files
issue: Three unenumerated triggers gating major execution-mode switch: 'service/page groups' undefined; 'explicit analysis tasks' — no detection rule; 'All plans touch same files' — ambiguous (any, full, >50% overlap?). If two_wave: true override set but detection says single-wave, no priority rule.
remediation_sketch: Replace heuristic triggers: 'Two-wave activates ONLY when two_wave: true in phase CONTEXT.md. Auto-detection disabled.' OR specify: 'Auto-activate when plan count >= 4 AND any plan has wave_role: set. The two_wave override ALWAYS wins.' Drop service/page groups prose.

excerpt: Section 5.7: SUMMARY.md Export Validation ... For each missing required section: - Log advisory warning ... This is advisory, NOT blocking
issue: Validator has no done state. After logging warnings, unclear when Section 5.7 is complete. Validation report location (Section 4 Step 6 wave completion vs 5.7 Step 5) is inconsistent — 4 Step 6 doesn't list validation as required section. Graceful-degradation philosophy collides with Principle 12 'verification-gated completion' — which wins for SUMMARY.md structural validation vs task verification?
remediation_sketch: Specify done state: 'Section 5.7 complete when (1) every SUMMARY.md read+checked; (2) validation line printed per plan to wave completion; (3) warnings captured in ## Advisories sub-section. Does not block wave; separate from Principle 12 task verification which DOES block.'

excerpt: Step 1.5: ... Extract the suffix after the last hyphen-delimited division prefix (e.g., 'dev-senior-developer' -> 'senior-developer', 'backend-architect' -> 'backend-architect') ... d) If MULTIPLE matches: pick the first ... e) If ZERO matches ... fall back to autonomous mode
issue: Fuzzy-match cascade has three defects: (1) division prefix list unenumerated; inconsistent examples ('backend-architect' -> 'backend-architect' implies 'backend' is not a prefix); (2) 'pick the first' is filesystem-order-dependent non-deterministic; (3) autonomous-mode fallback here contradicts Section 2 Step 5(f) and Section 6 Error #5 which both say STOP with error. Three sections give three instructions for same condition. Matches LEGION-47-052 defect class.
remediation_sketch: (a) Unify fallback across Sections 2, 3, 6 — pick STOP per Section 6 rationale. (b) Enumerate prefix list or reference agent-registry CATALOG.md. (c) Replace 'pick the first' with 'STOP: ambiguous agent-id matched N candidates'. (d) Consider removing fuzzy match entirely — it is the defect, not the fix.

excerpt: When worktree mode is active: Principle 8 (Files isolation per wave) is relaxed — worktrees provide filesystem-level isolation instead of plan-level file list disjointness
issue: Worktree mode silently relaxes Principle 8 safety invariant via single boolean execution.use_worktrees in settings.json. No human-approval gate per CLAUDE.md authority matrix. 4.7 sees 'when worktree mode is active' as unconditional state check — does not verify user consent. Authority boundary ambiguous.
remediation_sketch: Add gate: 'Worktree mode requires (a) execution.use_worktrees: true AND STATE.md ## Consent section worktree_mode_consent: true, OR (b) explicit --worktrees flag on current invocation. If neither, treat as false regardless of settings.' Add initial-activation prompt via adapter.ask_user.

excerpt: ## Section 6: INTENT-BASED PLAN FILTERING ... ## Step 3.5: APPLY INTENT FILTERS (conditional) ... ## Section 10: File Placement Utilities (ENV-04)
issue: Structural defect: file has TWO Section 6 headings (Error Scenarios L1370, INTENT-BASED PLAN FILTERING L1629). Jumps from Section 7 (L1486) to Section 10 (L1722); no Sections 8 or 9. 'Step 3.5: APPLY INTENT FILTERS' (L1679) belongs to Section 4 but lives below Section 7. References table L1596 lists Section 6 = Error Handling, contradicting the later Section 6 header. 4.7 scanning 'Section 6' gets two contradictory results.
remediation_sketch: Renumber: Section 6 INTENT-BASED -> Section 8, Section 10 -> Section 9. Or move intent-filtering into Section 2 (Plan Discovery) and file-placement into Appendix A. Move Step 3.5 back into Section 4. Audit all cross-file references to Section 6/8/9/10 and update.

excerpt: ## Section 7: Two-Wave Pattern ... Enhanced execution model with distinct Build/Analysis and Execution waves for maximum parallelism.
issue: Goal stated ('maximum parallelism') but operational intent not front-loaded. Mechanic described before reader knows WHY this particular partition is correct. No decision procedure for classifying plans into build/analysis/execution/remediation. wave_role frontmatter has no rule for who sets it or how. Compounds LEGION-47-102 schema conflict.
remediation_sketch: Front-load intent: 'GOAL: Section 7 for phases needing explicit architecture review between build and validation. Decision: (1) if any two plans must produce artifacts that other plans review before execution, use Section 7; (2) assign wave_role: by role.' Integrate with Section 2 wave map.

excerpt: 9. AGENT COMPLETES BUT DOESN'T REPORT ... Detection: Agent appears idle but no completion report was received ... If the agent still doesn't respond: infer the result from the filesystem
issue: 'Agent appears idle' has no quantified threshold. 'Wait for the agent's response' — how long? Inference fallback silently upgrades no-response to 'Complete with Warnings' potentially fooling downstream. Filesystem evidence approach does not distinguish 'completed and forgot' from 'partially completed and hung'.
remediation_sketch: Add thresholds: 'idle = no adapter.collect_results within 60s after last activity. Send follow-up and wait 30s more. Infer from filesystem ONLY IF files_modified complete AND all <verification> pass; else Status: Failed with inconclusive.'

excerpt: Part 1: Agent personality - Read the ENTIRE agent .md file: agents/{agent-id}.md - Capture as PERSONALITY_CONTENT ... Assemble the final prompt using this exact template: {PERSONALITY_CONTENT} ... {TASK_DESCRIPTION} {RESULT_INSTRUCTION}
issue: Re-filing S05 deferred cross-cut. model_tier declared in agent-registry:147, passed by phase-decomposer:459, set to execution by review-evaluators:1259 — but cli-dispatch Section 3 never reads it. Prompt template has 5 parts (PERSONALITY, TASK, RESULT, CONTROL, HANDOFF) with no model_tier lookup. Claude-Code adapter hardcodes model: {model_execution}. Net: all tiers collapse to sonnet. Maintainers adding model_tier accumulate dead metadata.
remediation_sketch: Path A: add Part 6 reading model_tier from agent frontmatter (default execution), map to adapter.model_{tier}, substitute into spawn row. Update adapters' spawn_agent_personality rows to use {model} not hardcoded {model_execution}. Path B: delete model_tier declarations. Path A is correct long-term.

excerpt: Read the ENTIRE agent .md file: agents/{agent-id}.md

excerpt: Step 2: Compute critique verdict. PASS: No schema BLOCKERs AND no critical risks AND no critical assumptions. CAUTION: 1-2 critical items (including schema warnings), all have clear mitigations. REWORK: Any schema BLOCKER OR any wave overlap BLOCKER OR 3+ critical items OR any item without clear mitigation
issue: Boundary cases underspecified. (1) 2 items where one lacks clear mitigation: CAUTION passes count clause AND REWORK passes no-mitigation clause — simultaneous. (2) 'Clear mitigation' has no acceptance test; Section 1 Step 5 produces mitigations but does not rank clarity. (3) '0 critical + schema WARNING' falls to PASS but 'Plan looks solid' message contradicts warnings. Peer to LEGION-47-055 bracketed-stub acceptance-criteria.
remediation_sketch: (a) Ordered rule chain with first-match: (1) any schema/wave BLOCKER → REWORK; (2) any critical without clear mitigation → REWORK; (3) ≥3 critical → REWORK; (4) 1-2 critical all mitigated OR schema WARNING → CAUTION; (5) else PASS. (b) Define clear mitigation: 'specifies (i) concrete action, (ii) responsible agent or autonomous step, (iii) verifiable outcome. Failing any → UNCLEAR → rule (2).' (c) PASS message: 'Plan looks solid' → 'Plan passes all blocking checks' (does not contradict warnings).

excerpt: Step 2: Check for existing spec. Look for .planning/specs/{NN}-{phase-slug}-spec.md. If exists: Inform user: 'Spec document already exists for Phase {N}.' Peer CAT-1 to LEGION-47-122, 117, 120.
remediation_sketch: (a) 4 options: Overwrite / Update (re-run Sections 4-5) / Keep existing / Cancel. (b) Staleness check: fingerprint phase_requirements+success_criteria from ROADMAP.md; add requirements_fingerprint field to spec template; if differs, mark Keep option '(WARNING: may be stale)' requiring acknowledgment. (c) Document Update trade-off vs. 'no merge' policy; if unsupported, mark 'Not supported in v7.4.0'.

Agent selection: prefer agents with domain expertise

(c) Concrete trigger: 'Spawn iff ANY of: (i) requirements reference tech not in CODEBASE.md detected-stack, (ii) phase goal matches domain_keywords registry, (iii) estimated_plans ≥ 3.' (d) Precondition check: 'If adapter.spawn_agent_readonly unavailable, skip spawning; orchestrator-self-research with note.'

excerpt: Step 3: Validate spec completeness. Before moving to Section 4, check: Every requirement from Section 1 appears in the Requirements table; Every deliverable has a defined path and purpose; Architecture section explains how deliverables connect; Key decisions have rationale (not arbitrary choices); Open questions are categorized as Blocking or Deferrable

'Open questions categorized' no verification of categories. 4.7 reader self-certifies PASS 100% with no false-negative signal. Gate nominal but does not gate. Same CAT-8 class as LEGION-47-126.
remediation_sketch: Replace each soft check: (a) 'Requirements table row count == Section 1 categorized count; report diff if mismatch.' (b) 'Each Deliverables entry has non-empty Path: and description; missing → FAIL.' (c) 'Architecture section has ≥ N-1 pairwise relationship statements where N = deliverable count, OR explicit Deliverables-independent note if N=1 or unlinked.' (d) 'Every Key Decision row has non-empty Rationale AND Alternatives Considered.' (e) 'Every Open Question row has Status ∈ {Blocking, Deferrable}.' Any fail: re-run Section 3 Step 2 with gap; do not proceed.

excerpt: Step 3: Rate overall complexity. Simple: 1-2 requirements, ≤3 deliverables, 1 wave | 1 plan likely. Medium: 2-3 requirements, 3-5 deliverables, 1-2 waves | 2 plans. Complex: 4+ requirements, 5+ deliverables, 2+ waves, architectural choices | 3+ plans
issue: Rating thresholds overlap at boundaries — '2-3 requirements' in both Simple (1-2) and Medium (2-3). Phase with 2 requirements, 3 deliverables, 1 wave can be Simple OR Medium equally. 'Architectural choices' undefined. Not safety-critical but response-calibration gap: same input different outputs across agent instances, undermining downstream Medium-Optional / Simple-Skip proposal gate. Peer to LEGION-47-086, 109.
remediation_sketch: (a) Disjoint thresholds first-match: 'Simple: 1 req AND ≤3 deliv AND 1 wave. Medium: 2-3 req OR 4-5 deliv OR 2 waves. Complex: ≥4 req OR ≥6 deliv OR ≥3 waves OR ≥1 architectural choice.' (b) Define architectural choice: 'determines directory structure, module boundaries, state management, or data schema — not mere implementation detail.' (c) Tie-breaker: 'Boundary case → select higher tier. Rationale: over-estimating surfaces proposals; under-estimating skips silently.'

excerpt: ### Stage 1: Vision & Identity (1-3 exchanges). Purpose: Capture {project_name}, {project_description}, {value_proposition}, {target_users}. Open with: 'What are you building? Give me the elevator pitch.' ... After Stage 1 — Summarize and confirm: 'Here's what I'm understanding: [project_name] is [project_description]. It's for [target_users] and the core value is [value_proposition]. Anything to correct or add?'

But Section 1 item 4 declares Stage 1-2 'conversational' — free-text capture. 4.7 reader faces contradiction: follow this skill (free-text) or follow CLAUDE.md (AskUserQuestion). Confirmation 'Anything to correct or add?' is unbounded free-text no AskUserQuestion schema can express. questioning-flow is ground zero — every consumer inherits. P1 escalation: user-facing, first-run failure class, canonical-skill propagation. Blocked on adapter.prompt_free_text primitive (S03 outstanding).
remediation_sketch: (a) Resolve adapter.prompt_free_text decision — add primitive to contract AND rewrite CLAUDE.md mandate to exempt or require it, OR reframe Stage 1 as bounded questions with 'Other (free-text)' escape hatches via primitive. (b) Revise Section 1 item 4 self-consistent: if mandated everywhere, 'conversational' not valid mode — restructure Stage 1 option-driven; if exempted, cite exemption and mechanism. (c) Replace 'Anything to correct or add?' with AskUserQuestion: Proceed / Correct specific field / Add missing info (free-text via primitive) / Cancel.

(b) Wrap prose in adapter.ask_user invocation: question='Execution mode?' options=['Guided (recommended)', 'Autonomous', 'Collaborative']. Persist to {decisions_table}. Do all three. (c) Non-interactive fallback: 'If non-interactive or timeout, apply defaults (Guided/Standard/Balanced); record rationale Defaulted in decisions_table.' excerpt: Adaptive follow-ups — select based on project type detected in Stage 1: | Code/software | Stack preferences? Existing codebase? ... | | Content/marketing | Platforms? Voice/tone? ... | | Design | Brand guidelines? ... | | Research/analysis | ... | | Mixed/unclear | Cover the most relevant subset; skip the rest |

Signals cross-cut with LEGION-47-115/116 domain-keyword registry gap — 'Content/marketing' maps to marketing, 'Design' maps to design, but no reference to canonical registry. 'Mixed/unclear' 'cover the most relevant subset' has no decision rule. S02c re-inheritance.

First match wins; ties → Mixed/unclear.' (b) Cross-reference canonical registry (blocked on 115/116 landing). excerpt: 5. Target 5-8 total exchanges — not 20 questions. Combine related questions. Skip what's already clear. [Section 4 Step 3] Size each phase for 2-3 plans (max 3 tasks per plan): Simple phase (config, setup): 1-2 plans; Standard phase (feature build): 2-3 plans; Complex phase (multi-system integration): 3 plans

(b) Stage completion: 'Stage 2 complete when requirements_list has ≥1 item AND out_of_scope explicitly acknowledged (confirm nothing-to-exclude if empty).' (c) Disjoint sizing: 'Simple: 1 plan. Standard: 2 plans. Complex: 3 plans. >3: split or cite exception.'

excerpt: ## Section 1: Questioning Philosophy. 1. Vision first, technology second ... 2. Adaptive depth — follow the user's energy. If they go deep on a topic, explore it. ... 3. Infer where possible, confirm where uncertain ... 4. Structured choices for preferences, open conversation for vision ... 5. Target 5-8 total exchanges — not 20 questions. ... 6. Summarize between stages
issue: Six principles stated with maximalist bold-headed framing invite absolute-rule reading but have internal conflicts. Principle 2 'follow energy' vs. Principle 5 '5-8 target' when user goes deep. Principle 3 'Infer where possible, confirm where uncertain' confidence threshold unspecified — reader may infer everything or confirm everything. CAT-5 over-reliance: guidance framed as rules, brittleness under unusual inputs.
remediation_sketch: (a) Reframe as defaults with explicit escapes: 'Vision first — ask identity before stack UNLESS user opens with stack choice, in which case capture, confirm, return to vision.' Each principle. (b) Principle 3 uncertainty: 'Inference permitted iff confidence ≥ 0.7 — inferred value matches user wording or strong signal. <0.7, confirm explicitly.' (c) 2 vs. 5 resolution: 'If user energy signals deep exploration (3+ follow-ups on same field), accept exceeding target; note in decisions_table Extended questioning — user requested depth.'

excerpt: ### Existing .planning/ directory detected. The start.md command handles the overwrite warning. If the user confirms reinitialize, proceed with a clean questioning flow. Do not attempt to merge with existing state — it's a fresh start.

If start.md cleanup incomplete, or user invokes questioning-flow directly (not via start.md — architecturally possible), skill writes into partially-populated state. excerpt: General formula for any board size N: 1. approve_count >= ceil(2 * N / 3) then APPROVED 2. approve_count > floor(N / 2) then APPROVED WITH CONDITIONS 3. approve_count == N / 2 (even N only) then ESCALATE to user 4. Otherwise then REJECTED ... N = 2 (under-population): 2-0 APPROVED (unanimous) | 1-1 ESCALATE (split) | 0-2 REJECTED
issue: Resolution formula has exit-condition state partition gap: at N=2, table omits 'APPROVED WITH CONDITIONS'. Reading formula at N=2: condition 1 (approve >= ceil(4/3)=2) fires only at 2-0. Condition 2 (approve > floor(2/2)=1) requires approve=2 — already captured by condition 1, so condition 2 unreachable at N=2. A 2-member board with both approving AND stating conditions collapses to APPROVED per L436. ABSTAIN path (L366-367) reduces N; if N=3 starts with abstain, effective N=2 — resolution never re-communicates change. Peer LEGION-47-054, 151, 161, 196.
remediation_sketch: Fix formula: condition 2 as 'approve > floor(N/2) AND approve < ceil(2N/3)' — strictly majority-not-supermajority. Add N=2 row: 'approve==2 AND any approver stated conditions then APPROVED WITH CONDITIONS.' Document ABSTAIN effect: 'When ABSTAIN reduces effective N, announce updated N to user before applying resolution table.' Add acceptance-criteria block: 'Resolution complete when verdict in {APPROVED, APPROVED WITH CONDITIONS, REJECTED, ESCALATED} AND written to MEETING.md AND tally persisted.'

External dispatch would mean rounds x board_members CLI calls (e.g., 2 rounds x 5 members = 10+ invocations), which is cost-prohibitive and slow. External CLI assessments from Phase 1 are the authoritative domain input; Phase 2 discussion refines and synthesizes based on those findings.
issue: 'Rationale for internal execution' placed mid-Section 3 as post-hoc explanation — a reader of Section 1 and Section 2 already saw 'Dispatch assessments (parallel)' with cli-dispatch option (L161-165) and expects Phase 2 to follow same pattern. Rationale appears only on reaching Section 3 Step 0 (L203). 4.7 reading Section 2 and beginning Phase 2 prep with cli-dispatch may attempt external dispatch before reaching L203. CAT-4 underspecified intent: WHY stated but deferred; WHAT (Phase 2 internal) should be front-loaded in Section 1 overview.
remediation_sketch: Move rationale to Section 1 as 'Execution Model Overview' block: 'Phase 1 (Assessment) and Phase 3 (Vote) support external CLI dispatch. Phase 2 (Discussion) and Phase 4 (Resolution) run internally — Phase 2 because external dispatch cost-prohibitive; Phase 4 because pure computation.' Remove duplicated explanation from Section 3 L203-209; one-line reference: 'Per Section 1 Execution Model, Phase 2 runs internally.'

excerpt: updateMappings(changes, currentMappings, significance): Step 1: Create backup ... Step 5: Report updates return { updated: true, backupPath: backupPath, changes: changes, significance: significance }
issue: Section 16 Auto-Update Protocol defines change detection + significance + update but no acceptance-criteria spec for when auto-update complete: (a) What happens when updated: true consumed by caller? (b) Is user notified via AskUserQuestion (mode: prompt L1890)? Closure? (c) L1885-1898 three modes — prompt/auto/disabled — but L1850 'Post-execution: Auto-detect if enabled' doesn't state what happens when mode=prompt and user says no: re-prompt next run, mark user-rejected, silently accept stale? excerpt: Graceful Degradation - If CODEBASE.md does not exist: all consumers skip brownfield context silently - If CODEBASE.md is stale (>30 days): consumers warn but do not block - Never error, never block, never require brownfield analysis for workflow completion ... 1. Check if .planning/CODEBASE.md exists 2. If yes: use codebase data ... 4. Never error on missing CODEBASE.md 5. Never block workflow completion 6. Never require brownfield analysis 7. Never auto-trigger analysis without user consent
issue: Two 'Never' clusters: L82 'Never error, never block, never require' (3 Nevers one line), L896-899 'Never error / Never block / Never require / Never auto-trigger' (4 Nevers). 7 Never instructions in close proximity — 4.7 may adopt over-defensive posture, refusing to surface CODEBASE.md-related warnings even when helpful (e.g., >180-day stale with detected structural changes worth warning with elevated prominence, but 'never block' + 'Never require' conflict with elevated signal).
remediation_sketch: L82 replace with positive: 'Workflow completion is independent of brownfield analysis. Missing or stale CODEBASE.md surfaces as advisory in /legion:status but does not change command exit status.' (3) Surface stale-CODEBASE.md only when user-visible (status, advise); other commands degrade silently. (4) Auto-analysis requires explicit user opt-in via AskUserQuestion; no background triggering.' Cross-reference LEGION-47-166.

(a) L157-162 'gaps = (what's needed) - (stated + implied)' is set-math with no operationalization: 'what's needed' undefined, 'implied' not a list; 4.7 enumerates gaps non-deterministically — same raw_concept + same answers → different gap sets. (b) L278-282 early-exit uses qualitative triggers: 'Crystallization achieved early' has no measurable threshold; 'Blocker discovered' has no definition (L185 blocker→park but L178-185 allows deferred — overlapping categories). (b) Operationalize early-exit: 'Early crystallization: all required items resolved by exchange 4 AND no open blockers → offer early decision via AskUserQuestion {Proceed now, Run remaining confirmatory exchanges}. Blocker = status=blocker OR deferred gap in Technical/Dependency categories when mode=crystallize.' Add acceptance-criteria at end of Section 5.

excerpt: The golden rule: every workflow that calls memory must work identically without it. ... 4. NEVER: a. Error if memory files are missing b. Block workflow execution on memory operations c. issue: Five-item NEVER: cluster stacked under single heading in skill's core contract, plus three additional Do NOT directives in Step 3 (L386-388). Peer LEGION-47-210 (codebase-mapper 7 Nevers), LEGION-47-177 (authority-enforcer DO NOT stack). 4.7 absorbs prohibitive clusters as taboo enumeration and adopts over-defensive posture. excerpt: Store Decision (embedded in outcome records): Key decisions are captured in the Summary and Tags fields of outcome records rather than a separate file. issue: 'Store Decision' is named operation with no input contract, no call-site, no trigger rule, no required-field list. Two examples but no rule for WHEN decisions embedded vs skipped. Under literalism: (a) engine never invokes because no procedural description, or (b) embeds every Summary with decision-flavored phrasing, inflating OUTCOMES.md with prose that confounds keyword-match in Section 4 recall. The agent-selection tag is NOT in Section 3 Step 4 valid task_types list — ambiguous whether task_type or free tag.
remediation_sketch: Elevate to full sub-section with (a) trigger criteria: 'Embed decision in Summary when outcome involves non-default agent selection OR cycle-count override OR user-corrective signal.' (b) Required phrasing: 'Decision summaries begin with Chose X over Y | Escalated after N cycles | Deferred to N so recall keyword matcher can pull with query_tag=decision.' (c) Add decision to task_types valid list OR clarify decision is free tag distinct from task_types. (d) Caller list: which workflows invoke Store Decision.

excerpt: Step 7: Verify - Confirm the new record appears in OUTCOMES.md - Confirm Task Type column is non-empty - If write failed: output the record as text to the user (never lose data) ... excerpt: Step 4: Validate and resolve task_type - task_type MUST be populated (never leave blank) 1. Primary: use the task_type passed by execution-tracker 2. CATALOG.md NOT in context window of /legion:build or /legion:review so L124 validation CANNOT run — agent falls through to Fallback 2 (general) on every record. (c) Section 4 Recall Archetype Scores (L258-306) consumes same Task Type column with no cross-reference to inference chain — if callers resolve differently, recall groups don't match store values. excerpt: Concurrent writes: Not a concern in Legion workflows — only one build/review runs at a time per project. If somehow two writes conflict, the second write wins (last-write-wins, consistent with all other Legion state files). ... Branch Detection: Run: git branch --show-current Result: branch name ... Fallback: if command fails or returns empty, use unknown
issue: Two preconditions with false-premise handling. (a) L416 'only one build/review runs at a time per project' contradicts wave-executor's parallel-dispatch model (Wave 1 parallel plans may record outcomes via execution-tracker simultaneously); last-write-wins falls back on filesystem-level atomicity NOT guaranteed for append-to-table (two concurrent Read→append→Write can lose records). Peer LEGION-47-082. (b) L786-787 git branch --show-current preconditions include: git installed, cwd in git repo, detached HEAD returns empty. No precondition check. If memory-manager invoked outside git worktree (/legion:portfolio reading moved/copied project), every record gets Branch=unknown silently — Section 11 Branch-Scoped Recall becomes useless.
remediation_sketch: (a) L416: 'Concurrent writes from parallel-dispatched agents are a real concern in Wave 2+ parallel plans. Store Outcome MUST acquire a file lock (flock .planning/memory/OUTCOMES.md.lock -c read+append+write) or serialize via execution-tracker Section 2 single-writer commit. Without locking, record loss is possible under parallel dispatch.' Add concurrent-store regression test. (b) L782-788: 'Precondition: git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree returns 0. If not, set branch=no-git and emit <escalation severity=info type=infrastructure> once per session. Do not silently substitute unknown — distinguish missing-git from detached-HEAD from worktree-not-a-repo.'
